# Formalization Board

Last updated: 2026-06-08.

This board is the source of truth for formalization status.  Percentages are
only informal summaries; the frontier below is determined by endpoint
signatures, local build status, and dependency inspections.

Protected constraints:

- Do not edit `PptFactorization/AppendixBSpikeLowerBound.lean`.
- Do not change `lean-toolchain`.
- Do not add `axiom`, `opaque`, `unsafe`, `admit`, `sorry`, or extra theorem
  parameters to hide gaps.
- Lean/mathlib foundational dependencies `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and
  `Quot.sound` are acceptable.

Status wording:

- "Checked" means a specific local artifact has been verified: the file builds,
  the stated theorem or adapter exists with the advertised statement, and the
  dependency inspection has the expected footprint.
- "Checked" does not mean the whole ticket is done.  A checked adapter can
  still expose theorem-strength inputs such as a moment estimate, a mixed-word
  bound, a survivor analysis, or a scalar limit.
- "Done" is reserved for a ticket whose theorem-facing inputs have all been
  supplied or eliminated, so that no remaining assumption in that ticket is
  carrying the intended mathematical content.
- "Audited from Lean/mathlib foundations" means the named Lean proof object has
  no project-specific axiom, `sorry`, `admit`, or `unsafe` dependency beyond
  the explicit hypotheses printed in its theorem statement and the standard
  foundational dependencies above.
- It does not mean the original upper or lower random-matrix theorem is
  unconditional.  If a theorem statement still assumes a growing-moment bound,
  survivor analysis, variance/Chebyshev estimate, or mixed-word frontier, that
  input is still live theorem-strength work.
- Avoid saying "from nothing."  The precise phrase is: "from explicit
  hypotheses, with the adapter proof audited to Lean/mathlib foundations."

## Publication Verdict

- Upper-tail theorem: autonomous at manuscript level in the balanced regime
  \(s(d)/d^2\to\lambda>0\), for fixed \(k\ge3\) and \(\varepsilon>0\).
- Background half-mass: folded into the paper as a natural-scale concentration
  theorem, using standard induced-state moment convergence and edge estimates.
- Local spike certificate: folded into the paper as a deterministic mixed-word
  estimate, with the \(P,C\) branch treated structurally.
- Two-sided statement: conditional on a separate lower-tail estimate.
- Lower proof in the combined Overleaf bundle: keep visibly conditional unless
  a separate lower-tail proof campaign is launched.
- Publication posture: autonomous upper-tail result, with optional conditional
  lower/two-sided discussion.

## 2026-06-05 Artifact Baseline

- Current paper commit: `33abdf3` made the upper theorem autonomous in the
  note.
- Canonical combined source:
  `../Overleaf_Input_Bundle/main.tex`, which inputs `upper_proof.tex` and
  `lower_proof.tex`.
- Delivery invariant: upper theorem remains autonomous; lower/two-sided
  material remains explicitly conditional.

## 2026-06-05 Current Aubrun Frontier

### Current Bidefect/U-AUB-154 Reduction Frontier (2026-06-08)

- Current honest endpoint:
  `AppendixB.AubrunBiDefectCountBound_of_bianeAdjacentLinkHalves_left_gap_three_start_impossible_frontiers_nestedStrictReturnIntervalOpenNonwrapOffCycleSmallerInternalOnly_and_chapuy`.
  It is a checked conditional reduction to `AubrunBiDefectCountBound m`, not a
  no-input closure of `U-AUB-02`.
- The theorem-facing leaves on this endpoint are exactly:
  large-gap left Biane outside `c-a=3`; large-gap right Biane outside
  `d-b=3, c=b+2`; the four remaining wrap-side internal-subinterval collision
  theorems (below-start-after-skipped, below-open-on-cycle-after-skipped,
  above-start-before-image, above-open-on-cycle-before-image);
  the fifteen explicit side-specific primitive branch collision theorems; and
  the Chapuy/trisection recurrence.  The smaller nonwrapping
  child branch is no longer theorem-facing: `U-AUB-358` absorbs strict child
  primitive residuals into the parent primitive leaf, and `U-AUB-359` closes
  the genuine child internal-subinterval branch by side-data-preserving
  well-founded descent.  `U-AUB-360` then splits the parent primitive leaf into
  the five explicit primitive branch leaves, and `U-AUB-361` splits the two
  wrap-side primitive leaves into ten more explicit primitive branch leaves.
  `U-AUB-362` splits the two wrap-side internal-subinterval leaves into
  start-touching and open-internal alternatives, and `U-AUB-363` splits the
  two wrap-open alternatives into off-skipped-cycle and on-skipped-cycle
  leaves.  `U-AUB-364` then position-refines the two on-cycle open leaves:
  the wrap-below on-cycle witness starts at or after `x`, and the wrap-above
  on-cycle witness starts at or before `π x`.  `U-AUB-365` gives the same
  side-forced position refinement for the two start-touching leaves.
  `U-AUB-366` replaces the two wrap off-cycle open leaves by strict smaller
  contour-repeat packets: the checked endpoint-same-cycle block theorem traps
  the whole witness block inside the parent interval and off the skipped
  `x`-cycle before the endpoint calls the theorem-facing collision input.
  `U-AUB-367` then splits those two strict smaller-contour packets internally:
  immediate common-cycle branches close, child primitive residual branches are
  absorbed by the parent primitive leaf, and only child internal-subinterval
  branches remain theorem-facing in those two wrap off-cycle positions.
  `U-AUB-368` closes those two wrap off-cycle child-internal branches
  internally: noncrossing upgrades the block-off-`x` invariant to a full
  no-skipped-cycle invariant on the child interval, and a generic well-founded
  descent consumes the child-internal leaf from the parent primitive supplier.
  `U-AUB-369` adds finite sanity coverage for the exact four remaining
  position-refined wrap leaves; through `n=9` those leaves have zero hits
  because no nonfixed predecessor skips occur in the enumerated left-tight
  slice.  This is tooling evidence only.
  The finite
  gap-three branch list is empty on
  this endpoint after `U-AUB-322`, and the direct-hit predecessor-skip branch is
  internal after `U-AUB-325`.  `U-AUB-326` adds a checked tied contour extractor
  for the nested skip leaf, `U-AUB-327` extracts first-return contour
  intervals in the nonwrapping and wrap-above nested branches, and `U-AUB-328`
  adds the wrap-below last-return interval plus a unified branch-normalized
  nested-return theorem.  `U-AUB-329` makes that return interval theorem-facing
  at the sharp endpoint.  `U-AUB-330` closes the terminal full-endpoint
  repeated-contour subcase, and `U-AUB-331` separates the remaining
  repeated-contour alternatives into proper endpoint-touching or internal
  cases.  `U-AUB-332` sharpens the endpoint-touching part by removing the
  start-adjacent branch and routing the end-adjacent branch to a fixed
  predecessor.  `U-AUB-333` makes this strict return frontier theorem-facing
  at the sharp endpoint, and `U-AUB-334` removes the already-solved common
  branch from that theorem-facing frontier.  `U-AUB-335` replaces the raw
  internal-right branch by the checked adjacent-or-strictly-shorter descent.
  `U-AUB-356` does the analogous adjacent-or-shorter split for the smaller
  child raw internal-`T` branch, and the current audited endpoint no longer
  exposes that raw `T` hypothesis.
  `U-AUB-358` proves that a primitive residual on a strict child interval is
  already a primitive residual on the parent interval; the parent primitive
  leaf now consumes the child internal `S`, start `S`, end `S`,
  fixed-predecessor, and adjacent `T` alternatives internally.
  `U-AUB-337` gives a conditional primitive descent, but `U-AUB-339` refutes
  its loose primitive input without side data, so `U-AUB-340` realigns the
  current honest endpoint to the side-tied descended residual route.
  The collision theorem itself is still open.
- The left-start gap-three slice `c-a=3, b=a+1` is now fully discharged at
  this endpoint by `U-AUB-312` through `U-AUB-316`.
- The right special-slice branch `S(b,c)` is discharged at this endpoint by

- The right `π(b+1,c)` branch is no longer independent: `U-AUB-318` routes it

- The right fixed `b+1` branch is no longer independent: `U-AUB-319` routes it
  through the same then-live `S(b+1,d)` branch by incidence-tree uniqueness.
- The remaining right `S(b+1,d)` branch is now closed by `U-AUB-320`: if
  `S(c,d)` failed, path uniqueness forces `π(b+1,c)`, the shifted left link
  gives `S(a,b)`, and the incidence-square obstruction contradicts the branch.
- The checked right gap-three special slice no longer has a theorem-facing
  branch at the sharp endpoint.
- The left fixed `a+1` end branch is now closed by `U-AUB-321`: fixedness
  gives `S(a+1,b)`, and a denial of `S(a,b)` creates a length-four path from
  `S(a)` to `S(c)` contradicting the direct length-two path from `π(a,c)`.
- The left-end `S(a+1,c)` branch is now closed by `U-AUB-322`: the copied
  contour step `a+1 -> b` and the reversed contour step `b -> c` give two
  simple paths from `S(c)` to `S(b)`, so tree uniqueness forces
  `π(a+1,b)`, and the existing `π(a+1,b)` absorber closes the target.

  Appendix B build, downstream Aristotle target build, the finite-branch

- 2026-06-06 strategic no-go warning from the pasted Motzkin analysis:
  do not try to close `U-AUB-02` by proving the uniform bound
  `Fintype.card (WickCyclePairTightClass m) ≤ 2^(m+1)`.  The pasted
  mathematical analysis identifies this simultaneous-geodesic class with
  noncrossing partial matchings, whose cardinality is Motzkin; that predicts a
  failure already at `m=7` (`M₈=323>256=2^8`).  This warning is not yet the
  formal Motzkin classification theorem in Lean, but it marks the current
  `m=3` geodesic-count route as deliberately non-uniform.
- 2026-06-06 checked zero-defect no-go adapter:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.not_forall_card_WickCyclePairTightClass_le_two_pow_of_formula_seven_eq_323`

  `Fintype.card (WickCyclePairTightClass m) ≤ 2^(m+1)` is impossible once the

  false uniform zero-defect envelope from being treated as a viable `U-AUB-02`

- 2026-06-06 auxiliary bidefect route checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPlusDefect` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickMinusDefect` split the Cayley defect into
  the two oriented deficits.  The checked theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPlusDefect_add_wickMinusDefect_eq_wickDefect`
  proves that their sum is the old total `wickDefect`.  The new bidefect slice
  type `PptFactorization.AppendixB.WickBiDefectClass`, count
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickBiDefectCount`, and envelope
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.aubrunBiDefectCountEnvelope` expose the intended

  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.AubrunBiDefectCountBound`.  The bridge

  count/fiber package over `Δ≤2m`.
- 2026-06-06 bidefect scalar checkpoint:
  the scalar absorption endpoint is now checked.  The helper
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.binomialWeightedSum_le_shiftedBase` proves
  \[
  \sum_a {N \choose a}P^a x^{N-a}\le (x+Q)^N
  \]
  whenever `0≤P`, `0≤x`, and `P≤Q`.  Applying this once with
  `N=m+3`, `x=d`, and once with `N=m+1`, `x=S`, the theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.aubrunBiDefectScalarSum_le_shiftedBases`
  absorbs the two bidefect binomial sums into
  \[
  2^{2(m+1)}(d+Q)^{m+3}(S+Q)^{m+1}.
  \]
  Build and audit pass with only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.
  The remaining bidefect debt is the actual count theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.AubrunBiDefectCountBound`; this is auxiliary to,
  not a closure of, the current tight-range `U-AUB-02` endpoint.
- 2026-06-06 bidefect double-sum factorization:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.aubrunBiDefectEnvelopeDoubleSum` is the exact
  double scalar sum obtained from the bidefect count envelope after assigning
  plus-defect powers to the first analytic base and minus-defect powers to the
  second.  The checked theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.aubrunBiDefectEnvelopeDoubleSum_eq_scalarSum`
  proves that this double sum factors exactly as
  \[
  \mathrm{aubrunBiDefectScalarSum}\bigl(m,P(2(m+1)),d,S\bigr).
  \]
  Together with `aubrunBiDefectScalarSum_le_shiftedBases`, the scalar side of
  the auxiliary bidefect route is now algebraically closed.  The remaining
  theorem-strength debt for that side route is the actual bidefect count theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.AubrunBiDefectCountBound`, plus the constant
  normalization needed before it could be compared with the current graduate
  endpoint.
- 2026-06-06 direct bidefect scalar endpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.aubrunBiDefectCountDoubleSum_le_envelopeDoubleSum`
  bounds the actual bidefect-count double sum by the envelope double sum from
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.AubrunBiDefectCountBound`.  The theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.aubrunBiDefectCountDoubleSum_le_shiftedBases`
  then combines the count-bound step, the exact factorization, and binomial
  absorption.  The canonical specialization
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.aubrunBiDefectCountDoubleSum_le_shiftedBases_Qone`
  uses `Q₁(2(m+1))`, relying on the checked domination `P(k)≤Q₁(k)`.
  This removes scalar plumbing from the auxiliary bidefect route.  It should not
  be treated as the active closure of `U-AUB-02` in the current ledger: the live
  theorem-facing input remains the tight-range fixed-defect count/fiber package,
  starting with the uniform zero-defect count audit.
- 2026-06-06 package-form exact-square endpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.eventually_gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_tightFiberPackages_exactSquare_Qone`
  is now checked in `PptFactorization.AppendixBTightFiberPackageEndpoint`.  It
  consumes exactly an eventual family
  `∀ Δ∈range(2m(d)+1), TightAubrunInnovationFiberPackage m(d) Δ` and supplies
  internally the exact-square close-window, positivity, ratio-model, and
  `P(k(d))≤d` side conditions.  This is endpoint wiring, not a proof of the
  package.  The remaining theorem-strength `U-AUB-02` work is to construct the
  packages uniformly for every `m` and every tight-range defect `Δ≤2m`.
- 2026-06-06 universal-package guardrail:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.not_forall_tightFiberPackage_of_formula_seven_eq_323`
  proves that if the `m=7` formula count is `323`, then the current
  fixed-defect-envelope version of
  `∀ m Δ∈range(2m+1), Nonempty (TightAubrunInnovationFiberPackage m Δ)`
  cannot exist.
  Thus the instruction "prove the package for all `m,Δ≤2m`" is not a valid
  final closure in the current API unless the zero-defect envelope/package is
  changed; the active mathematical leaf is still the `m=7`/Motzkin
  zero-defect classification or a redesigned scalar/count package.
- 2026-06-06 Motzkin arithmetic checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.motzkinNumber` is now defined by the standard
  first-return recurrence, and the small-value frontier is checked without
  native computation:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.motzkinNumber_eight` proves `M₈=323`, while
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.two_pow_eight_lt_motzkinNumber_eight` proves
  `2^8<M₈`.  The guardrail
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.not_forall_tightFiberPackage_of_formula_seven_eq_motzkinNumber_eight`
  now phrases the old-envelope contradiction in Motzkin form.  This is only
  scaffold for `U-AUB-122`: the missing theorem is still the classification
  `Fintype.card (WickCyclePairTightClassFormula 7)=motzkinNumber 8`.
- 2026-06-06 noncrossing-matching classification interface:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm n` now names the
  involutive, linearly noncrossing permutation model of partial matchings.
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.cyclePairTightClassFormulaEquivLinearNoncrossingMatching`
  proves that a pointwise iff between the cycle-pair tight predicate and this
  noncrossing-matching predicate gives an equivalence of subtypes.  The checked
  bridge
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_WickCyclePairTightClassFormula_eq_motzkinNumber_of_linearNoncrossing`
  then reduces the desired formula count to the Motzkin enumeration of
  `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm`.  For `m=7`, the no-go is now fed by
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.not_forall_tightFiberPackage_of_linearNoncrossingClassification_seven`.
  Remaining `U-AUB-122` content: prove the pointwise tight/noncrossing iff and
  prove `Fintype.card (LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm 8)=motzkinNumber 8`.
- 2026-06-06 Motzkin-code certificate target:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.MotzkinCode n` is now the canonical finite type
  `Fin (motzkinNumber n)`, with checked cardinal theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_MotzkinCode`.  The bridge
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm_eq_motzkinNumber_of_equivMotzkinCode`
  says that an explicit equivalence
  `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm n ≃ MotzkinCode n` proves the Motzkin
  enumeration.  For `m=7`, this feeds
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.not_forall_tightFiberPackage_of_linearNoncrossingEquivMotzkinCode_seven`.
  Remaining `U-AUB-122` content is now sharply split into: construct this
  equivalence at `n=8`, and prove the pointwise tight/noncrossing iff.
- 2026-06-06 small noncrossing-count sanity checks:
  the new noncrossing-matching model has kernel-checked Motzkin counts in the
  first nontrivial sizes:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm_four` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm_five`.
  Direct kernel enumeration at `n=6` was intentionally left out of the normal
  build path because it takes roughly a minute even with raised recursion
  depth; the `n=8` leaf still needs a structural equivalence or a hand
  certificate, not brute-force enumeration.
- 2026-06-06 small pointwise classification sanity check:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.cyclePairTightFormulaPredicate_iff_linearNoncrossing_three`
  proves, by kernel `decide`, that at `m=3` the cycle-pair tight formula
  predicate is exactly the involutive linearly noncrossing permutation
  predicate.  This attacks the other half of `U-AUB-122`.  Direct brute force
  for `m=4` was tested and works only with raised recursion depth and around
  48 seconds, so it is deliberately not in the normal build path.
- 2026-06-06 first structural Motzkin/count consistency checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_WickCyclePairTightClassFormula_three_eq_motzkinNumber_four`
  combines the `m=3` pointwise tight/noncrossing classification with the
  checked noncrossing-matching count at `n=4`.  The numerical corollary
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_WickCyclePairTightClassFormula_three_eq_nine_from_noncrossing`
  recovers the already-closed first nontrivial simultaneous-geodesic count
  through the new Motzkin route.  This verifies that the route has the right
  shape at `m=3`; it does not close the `m=7` count or the uniform Motzkin
  classification.
- 2026-06-06 noncrossing first-return interval closure:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.isLinearNoncrossingPerm_first_pair_left_interval_closed`
  proves that if vertex `0` is matched to `b`, then no vertex strictly between
  them is matched to the right of `b`.  The companion
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.isLinearNoncrossingPerm_first_pair_right_interval_closed`
  proves the right-side closure, using involutivity.  The fixed-point branch
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.isInvolutionPerm_first_fixed_tail_closed` proves
  that if `0` is fixed, then the positive tail is closed.  These are the
  structural interval facts needed for the Motzkin first-return decomposition of
  `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm`; they replace brute-force enumeration pressure
  with a recursive route toward `U-AUB-122`.
- 2026-06-06 noncrossing middle-block package:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.isInvolutionPerm_first_pair_partner_zero` packages
  the involutive symmetry of the first pair, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.isLinearNoncrossingPerm_first_pair_middle_interval_closed`
  proves that the open interval `(0,b)` is mapped into itself whenever
  `π 0=b`.  This is the exact left-block stability statement needed before
  constructing the recursive restriction of a noncrossing matching.
- 2026-06-06 fixed-tail recursive restriction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedTailPerm` constructs the permutation on
  `Fin n` obtained by deleting a fixed first vertex.  The characterization
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedTailPerm_succ_apply` lets later proofs
  transport statements back to the original permutation.  The preservation
  lemmas `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedTailPerm_isInvolutionPerm` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedTailPerm_isLinearNoncrossingPerm`
  package the fixed branch as
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedTailMatching :
    LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm (n+1) → LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm n`
  under the hypothesis `π 0=0`.  This closes the fixed-point half of the
  recursive construction; the paired-first branch and final enumeration remain
  open.
- 2026-06-06 fixed-first recursive extension:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedExtendPerm` extends a tail permutation
  to `Fin (n+1)` by fixing `0` and shifting the tail action by `Fin.succ`.
  The preservation lemmas
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedExtendPerm_isInvolutionPerm` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedExtendPerm_isLinearNoncrossingPerm`
  package this as
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedExtendMatching :
    LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm n → LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm (n+1)`.
  This supplies the inverse construction for the fixed branch; the next local
  step is proving that extension and restriction are inverse on the fixed
  branch.
- 2026-06-06 fixed-branch inverse laws:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedTailPerm_firstFixedExtendPerm` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedExtendPerm_firstFixedTailPerm` prove
  that fixed extension and tail restriction are inverse at the permutation
  level.  The matching-level theorems
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedTailMatching_firstFixedExtendMatching`
  and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstFixedExtendMatching_firstFixedTailMatching`
  lift this to `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm`.  Thus the fixed-point branch of
  the Motzkin first-return decomposition is now a checked equivalence component;
  the paired-first branch remains the active recursive-construction target.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch block restrictions:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.FirstPairMiddleBlock b` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.FirstPairRightBlock b` name the two stable
  subtypes in the branch `π 0=b`, `0<b`.  The restrictions
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddlePerm` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightPerm` are checked permutations of
  those subtypes, using the interval-closure lemmas from the previous round.
  This turns the paired branch from closure facts into actual restricted
  permutation data.  The next step is to re-index these subtypes by `Fin`
  cardinalities and build the paired-branch extension/inverse laws.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch finite re-indexing:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleBlockEquivFin` identifies the
  middle block `(0,b)` with `Fin (b.val-1)`, while
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightBlockEquivFin` identifies the right
  block after `b` with `Fin (n-b.val)`.  The cardinality lemmas
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_firstPairMiddleBlock` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_firstPairRightBlock` record the exact block
  sizes.  This prepares the paired branch for transport into canonical
  `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm` factors.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch canonical factors:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.transportPerm` transports permutations across
  equivalences, and the paired restrictions are now transported through the
  finite block equivalences as
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleIndexedPerm` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightIndexedPerm`.  The preservation
  lemmas
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleIndexedPerm_isInvolutionPerm`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleIndexedPerm_isLinearNoncrossingPerm`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightIndexedPerm_isInvolutionPerm`, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightIndexedPerm_isLinearNoncrossingPerm`
  show that both factors are still noncrossing involutions.  The packaged
  factors
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleIndexedMatching` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightIndexedMatching` now land in the
  canonical types `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm (b.val-1)` and
  `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm (n-b.val)`.  The next local step is the
  paired-branch extension construction and its inverse laws.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch extension equations:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleEmbed` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightEmbed` include the canonical
  recursive factors back into the ambient `Fin (n+1)` line.  The function
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendFun` swaps `0` with the partner
  `b`, applies the middle factor inside `(0,b)`, and applies the right factor
  after `b`.  The block equations
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendFun_zero`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendFun_partner`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendFun_middle`, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendFun_right` are checked.  This is
  the inverse paired-branch skeleton needed before proving the permutation,
  noncrossing, and inverse-law packaging.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch permutation package:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendFun_left_inv` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendFun_right_inv` prove that the
  paired extension and its recursive inverse undo each other.  The construction
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm` packages this as a
  permutation of `Fin (n+1)`, with computation rules
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_zero`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_partner`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_middle`, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_right`.  The theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_isInvolutionPerm` proves
  involutivity from involutive middle/right factors.  The remaining paired
  branch leaf is preservation of linear noncrossing.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch matching package:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleEmbed_lt_iff` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightEmbed_lt_iff` transport order
  comparisons between ambient positions and canonical recursive coordinates.
  The interval lemmas
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_middle_interval` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_right_interval` show the
  extension keeps middle vertices in the middle block and right vertices in the
  right block.  The theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_isLinearNoncrossingPerm`
  proves preservation of linear noncrossing, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendMatching` packages the paired
  extension as `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm (n+1)`.  The remaining paired
  branch task is the restriction/extension inverse laws.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch extension-to-restriction inverse laws:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleIndexedPerm_firstPairExtendPerm`
  and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightIndexedPerm_firstPairExtendPerm`
  prove that if one extends a middle/right pair across a positive first
  partner `b`, then restricting the result back to the middle and right
  canonical blocks recovers the two original permutation factors.  The
  matching-level lifts
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairMiddleIndexedMatching_firstPairExtendMatching`
  and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairRightIndexedMatching_firstPairExtendMatching`
  give the same inverse laws in `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm`.  The remaining
  paired-branch task is the reverse inverse law: start with a paired matching,
  restrict it to the two recursive factors, extend those factors, and recover
  the original matching.
- 2026-06-06 paired-branch restriction-to-extension inverse law:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendPerm_firstPairIndexedPerms` proves
  the reverse permutation-level inverse.  Starting with a noncrossing
  involution satisfying `π 0=b` and `0<b`, it restricts `π` to the middle and
  right canonical factors, extends those factors back across the first pair,
  and recovers `π` on all four regions: `0`, `b`, `(0,b)`, and the right
  block.  The matching-level theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.firstPairExtendMatching_firstPairIndexedMatchings`
  lifts this to `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm`.  The fixed and paired
  first-return inverse laws are now both checked; the remaining Motzkin task is
  to assemble them into a recursive equivalence/enumeration.
- 2026-06-06 noncrossing first-return equivalence:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.LinearNoncrossingFirstReturnDecomposition n`
  names the recursive Motzkin target: either `0` is fixed and one keeps a
  matching on the tail `Fin n`, or `0` is paired with a positive partner `b`,
  leaving a middle matching on `Fin (b.val-1)` and a right matching on
  `Fin (n-b.val)`.  The checked equivalence
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.linearNoncrossingFirstReturnEquiv n` assembles
  all fixed and paired branch inverse laws into
  `LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm (n+1) ≃
    LinearNoncrossingFirstReturnDecomposition n`.  This is the structural
  heart of the Motzkin enumeration; the remaining task is to turn this
  equivalence into the cardinal recurrence
  `card(n+1)=card(n)+Σ_i card(i) card(n-1-i)`, then identify it with
  `motzkinNumber`.
- 2026-06-06 noncrossing first-return cardinal recurrence:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm_succ_firstReturn`
  converts `linearNoncrossingFirstReturnEquiv` into the checked cardinal
  identity
  `card LNC(n+1)=card LNC(n)+Σ_b card LNC(b.val-1)*card LNC(n-b.val)`,
  where the sum is over positive partners `b : Fin(n+1)`.  This is the Motzkin
  recurrence before the final re-indexing `i=b.val-1`.  The remaining task is
  to re-index this sum by `Fin n` and then prove
  `Fintype.card (LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm n)=motzkinNumber n` by
  induction.
- 2026-06-06 Motzkin re-indexing and enumeration:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.positiveFinSuccEquiv n` identifies positive
  partners `b : Fin(n+1)` with `i : Fin n` by `i=b.val-1`.  The checked
  theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm_succ_motzkinIndexed`
  rewrites the first-return recurrence as
  `card LNC(n+1)=card LNC(n)+Σ_i card LNC(i)*card LNC(n-1-i)`.  From that,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_LinearNoncrossingMatchingPerm_eq_motzkinNumber`
  proves the Motzkin enumeration of linearly noncrossing matchings for every
  `n`.  The Motzkin-enumeration half of `U-AUB-122` is therefore closed; the
  remaining zero-defect diagnostic leaf is the pointwise classification
  `cyclePairTightFormulaPredicate m π ↔ isInvolutionPerm π ∧
  isLinearNoncrossingPerm π`, at least for `m=7` and preferably uniformly.
- 2026-06-06 Aubrun compilation hygiene:
  the current target `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBAubrunGraduate`
  completes without warnings.  The cleanup added local `omit` guards for unused
  section variables in the Aubrun import chain and removed one stale `simp`
  argument; no theorem statements were weakened.
- 2026-06-06 one-sided genus-slice checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.WickPlusGenusClass m g` names the plus-defect
  slice `wickPlusDefect π = 2*g`, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPlusDefectCount_even_eq_genusCount` proves
  that the even plus-defect count is exactly the corresponding genus count.
  This aligns the active `U-AUB-154` route with the unicellular-map/Chapuy
  proof strategy.  It does not prove Chapuy slicing or close `U-AUB-154`.
- 2026-06-06 plus-defect parity and genus reduction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPlusDefect_even` proves the one-sided defect
  is always even, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPlusDefectCount_odd_eq_zero` proves odd
  slices are empty.  `wickPlusGenusCount_zero_eq_card_leftTightClass` identifies
  genus zero with the one-sided left-tight geodesic class, while
  `wickPlusGenusCount_le_four_pow_mul_poly_of_zero_and_step` and
  `wickPlusDefectCount_real_le_commonEnvelope_of_genus_bounds` show that
  genus-zero Catalan plus Chapuy slicing imply the requested one-sided
  envelope.  The remaining hard leaves are now explicitly
  `wickPlusGenusCount_zero_le_four_pow` and `wickPlusGenusCount_succ_le`.
- 2026-06-06 genus-zero to noncrossing partition reduction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTightClassToNCPart_of_nonCrossing`,
  `card_leftTightClass_le_NCPart_of_nonCrossing_injective`, and
  `wickPlusGenusCount_zero_le_four_pow_of_NCPart_count` reduce the genus-zero
  base to exact `NCPart` inputs.  The base count now needs three theorem
  leaves: noncrossing of `leftTightCyclePartitionRange`, injectivity of that
  encoding, and a general cardinal bound for `NCPart (m+1)`.  The separate
  Chapuy recurrence `wickPlusGenusCount_succ_le` remains open.
- 2026-06-06 incidence-square obstruction checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.simpleGraph_isAcyclic_not_square` proves that an
  acyclic simple graph has no embedded four-cycle with distinct opposite
  vertices.  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_incidenceGraph_not_square`
  applies this to the Biane incidence graph of a one-sided Cayley-geodesic
  permutation.  The genus-zero noncrossing leaf is now narrowed to the
  permutation-theoretic step that converts a crossing of two left-tight cycle
  classes into such an incidence square.  This does not close
  `wickPlusGenusCount_zero_le_four_pow`; the `NCPart` cardinality,
  injectivity, and Chapuy recurrence leaves remain open.
- 2026-06-06 point-level incidence-square checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_forbid_point_incidence_square` proves
  the exact point-level contradiction consumed by the crossing proof: two
  `π`-cycle links and two `(finRotate*(π))`-cycle links forming an alternating
  square are impossible in the left-tight case when the opposite quotient
  vertices are distinct.  The remaining noncrossing blocker is now the Biane
  permutation lemma that a cyclic crossing of `π`-cycle classes supplies those
  two `(finRotate*(π))` links, or an equivalent strict cycle-count argument.
- 2026-06-06 Biane crossing-link reduction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTightCyclePartitionRange_nonCrossing_of_bianeCrossingLinks`
  proves the full `NCPartition.NonCrossing` goal from the exact crossing-link
  statement consumed by `leftTight_forbid_point_incidence_square`.  The active
  genus-zero noncrossing blocker is therefore no longer graph-theoretic: it is
  the permutation theorem that a genuine cyclic crossing of two `π` cycle
  classes in a left-tight geodesic supplies the two `(finRotate*(π))` cycle
  links and the separating non-link.  Injectivity of the encoding, the general
  `NCPart` cardinal bound, and Chapuy slicing remain separate open leaves.
- 2026-06-06 adjacent-link sharpening:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_not_sameCycle_both_of_lt` proves that
  a nontrivial `π`-cycle link cannot also be a `(finRotate * π)`-cycle link in
  the left-tight case.  Consequently
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTightCyclePartitionRange_nonCrossing_of_bianeAdjacentLinks`
  reduces the Biane noncrossing leaf to just the two adjacent links: for every
  genuine cyclic crossing `a<b<c<d`, prove
  `(finRotate * π).SameCycle a b` and `(finRotate * π).SameCycle c d`.  The
  separating non-link is now automatic; injectivity, the `NCPart` cardinal
  bound, and Chapuy slicing remain separate open leaves.
- 2026-06-06 crude `NCPart` count reduction:
  `PptFactorization.NCPartition.NCPart.minMaxCode` now records, for each point
  of a noncrossing partition, whether it is the minimum and/or maximum of its
  block.  The checked theorem
  `NCPartition.card_NCPart_le_four_pow_of_minMaxCode_injective` proves
  `Fintype.card (NCPartition.NCPart n) ≤ 4^n` from injectivity of this code.
  This avoids needing the exact Catalan count for the genus-zero bound, but the
  injectivity of `NCPart.minMaxCode` itself is still open.  It does not prove
  `wickPlusGenusCount_zero_le_four_pow`.
- 2026-06-06 min/max-code structural lemmas:
  `NCPartition.NonCrossing.block_element_le_right_of_between` and
  `NCPartition.NonCrossing.left_le_block_element_of_between` prove the core
  interval-nesting obstruction: a distinct block cannot pass through a
  bracketing pair of another noncrossing block.  The companion uniqueness
  lemmas `NCPart.eq_of_isBlockMin_of_same_part` and
  `NCPart.eq_of_isBlockMax_of_same_part` prove that each block has at most one
  minimum marker and at most one maximum marker.  These are checked ingredients
  for the future proof that `NCPart.minMaxCode` is injective; they do not close
  that injectivity theorem.
- 2026-06-06 min/max marker endpoint API:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_isBlockMin_mem_part` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_isBlockMax_mem_part` prove that every block has a
  concrete minimum and maximum marker, represented as `Fin n` points.  The
  unique-marker theorems `existsUnique_isBlockMin_mem_part` and
  `existsUnique_isBlockMax_mem_part` package uniqueness inside a block, and
  `isBlockMin_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` plus `isBlockMax_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq`
  transport marker predicates across equal min/max codes.  This is checked
  endpoint/reconstruction infrastructure for the `NCPart.minMaxCode`
  injectivity leaf; the injectivity theorem itself remains open.
- 2026-06-06 contour interval checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRotate_pow_apply_of_add_lt` identifies the
  no-wrap powers of the long rotation, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_incidenceLineGraph_contour_interval_reachable`
  turns the iterated contour step into concrete interval reachability between
  incidence edges.  This is bookkeeping for the Biane adjacent-link theorem;
  it does not prove `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`, and the genus-zero Catalan
  base remains open.
- 2026-06-06 incidence-tree path uniqueness:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_incidenceGraph_path_unique` and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_incidenceGraph_existsUnique_isPath`
  expose the tree uniqueness principle for the left-tight incidence graph:
  between any two incidence vertices there is a unique simple path/simple
  walk.  This is the graph comparison principle needed by the Biane contour
  argument; `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks` remains the next theorem-strength
  blocker.
- 2026-06-06 direct incidence path for a cycle link:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.setoidIncidenceGraph_left_walk_of_right_rel`
  builds the concrete two-edge incidence walk attached to a right-setoid link,
  while `setoidIncidenceGraph_left_walk_of_right_rel_isPath` and
  `setoidIncidenceGraph_left_walk_of_right_rel_length` prove that, when the
  left classes are distinct, this direct walk is a simple path of length two.
  This is the direct path to be compared with the contour path in the
  `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks` proof; the adjacent-link theorem itself
  remains open.
- 2026-06-06 dual direct incidence path:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.setoidIncidenceGraph_right_walk_of_left_rel`
  builds the symmetric two-edge incidence walk attached to a left-setoid link,
  and `setoidIncidenceGraph_right_walk_of_left_rel_isPath` plus
  `setoidIncidenceGraph_right_walk_of_left_rel_length` prove the corresponding
  simple-path and length-two facts when the right endpoints differ.  The
  adjacent-link proof now has both direct path orientations available.
- 2026-06-06 natural-index dual direct path:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_not_right_sameCycle_of_left_sameCycle_lt`
  proves the symmetric left-tight transversality fact: a nontrivial
  `(finRotate * π)` link cannot also be a `π` link.  The natural-index
  direct-path package
  `leftTight_incidenceGraph_right_walk_of_sameCycle`,
  `leftTight_incidenceGraph_right_walk_of_sameCycle_isPath`,
  `leftTight_incidenceGraph_right_walk_of_sameCycle_length`, and
  `leftTight_incidenceGraph_right_walk_unique_of_sameCycle` now gives the
  direct length-two path and uniqueness comparison between the right quotient
  vertices.  This closes the missing symmetric direct-path toolkit for the
  endpoint-touching branch, but it does not prove `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`.
- 2026-06-06 original-incidence contour step:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_step_left_walk`
  moves the Biane contour step from the line graph back into the original
  incidence tree: the step from `x` to `finRotate x` is the direct two-edge
  path through the `π`-class of `x` and `π⁻¹x`, with the endpoint transported
  along the `(finRotate * π)` class.  The accompanying `length` and `IsPath`
  lemmas make it ready for comparison with direct paths via tree uniqueness.
- 2026-06-06 iterated incidence contour:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_pow_left_walk`
  iterates the original-incidence contour step to a walk from `x` to
  `finRotate^n x` in the incidence tree.  The checked length formula
  `finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_pow_left_walk_length` gives length
  `2n`, and `finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_pow_left_reachable` exposes
  the corresponding tree reachability.  This prepares the interval/crossing
  comparison needed for `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`.
- 2026-06-06 intervalized incidence contour:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_interval_left_walk`
  specializes the iterated contour to ordinary intervals `a≤c` in
  `{0,…,m}`, using the no-wrap rotation formula.  The accompanying length and
  reachability lemmas give a concrete incidence-tree walk from the left
  quotient vertex over `a` to the one over `c`, of length `2(c-a)`.
- 2026-06-06 direct `π`-link path and contour comparison:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_incidenceGraph_left_walk_of_sameCycle`
  specializes the direct two-edge incidence path to natural indices `a<c`
  in the same `π`-cycle.  The checked `IsPath`, `length`, and uniqueness
  lemmas identify this as the unique simple path in the left-tight incidence
  tree.  The new comparison
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_contour_interval_eq_direct_of_sameCycle_isPath`
  says that, if the long-cycle interval contour between such endpoints is
  simple, it is exactly the direct path; consequently
  `leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_isPath_sub_eq_one` proves a simple
  same-cycle interval contour can only have `c-a=1`.  This turns longer
  same-cycle intervals into an explicit repeated-vertex problem, the next
  extraction step for `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`.
- 2026-06-06 longer same-cycle interval obstruction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_not_isPath_of_one_lt_sub`
  is the contrapositive of the direct-path comparison: a same-cycle interval
  with `1 < c-a` cannot be a simple contour path.  The companion
  `leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_support_not_nodup_of_one_lt_sub`
  exposes this as non-nodup support in the incidence tree.  The remaining
  theorem-strength step is to turn this repeated support into the adjacent
  `(finRotate * π)` links required by `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`.
- 2026-06-06 repeated contour-time witness:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_exists_repeated_getVert_of_one_lt_sub`
  strengthens non-nodup support to an explicit pair of contour times
  `i<j≤length` with equal `getVert` values.  This gives the adjacent-link
  proof a concrete witness to classify, rather than a bare failure of
  simplicity.
- 2026-06-06 contour-time coordinate formulas:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_step_left_walk_getVert_zero/one/two`
  identify the vertices of one contour step.  The iterated formulas
  `finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_pow_left_walk_getVert_even` and
  `..._odd` identify even contour times as left `(finRotate * π)` classes and
  odd contour times as right `π` classes; the interval formulas rewrite these
  classes to ordinary indices `a+r`.
- 2026-06-06 parity-classified repeated contour obstruction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRotate_mul_perm_incidence_contour_interval_left_walk_even_eq_sameCycle`
  and `..._odd_eq_sameCycle` translate equal same-parity contour vertices into
  explicit same-cycle equalities, while `..._even_ne_odd` rules out mixed
  parity.  The packaged theorem
  `leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_exists_repeated_parity_sameCycle_of_one_lt_sub`
  now turns every longer same-cycle interval into either a repeated
  `(finRotate * π)` class at two even contour times or a repeated `π` class at
  two odd contour times.  The active genus-zero blocker remains
  `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`.
- 2026-06-06 minimal-interval branch:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_exists_left_repeat_of_no_internal_right_repeat`
  proves the branch used in a minimal-crossing argument: once a longer
  same-cycle interval has no strictly internal repeated `π` class, the
  repeated contour obstruction is forced to be a repeated `(finRotate * π)`
  class.  The remaining theorem-strength work is to turn this repeated left
  class into the boundary adjacent link required by
  `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`.
- 2026-06-06 endpoint-touching extraction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_exists_left_repeat_touching_endpoint`
  sharpens the minimal-interval branch.  If the interval has neither an
  internal repeated `π` class nor an internal repeated `(finRotate * π)` class,
  the forced repeated left class must touch one endpoint.  The active
  theorem-strength leaf is still `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`: the remaining
  missing step is to identify that endpoint-touching repeat with the required
  adjacent boundary pair in a genuine crossing.
- 2026-06-06 left-repeat trichotomy:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_left_repeat_trichotomy_of_no_internal_right_repeat`
  is the broader checked case split behind endpoint-touching.  Under the
  no-internal-right-repeat hypothesis, the forced `(finRotate * π)` repeat is
  now classified as left-endpoint, right-endpoint, or genuinely internal.  The
  endpoint-touching theorem consumes this trichotomy plus the
  no-internal-left-repeat hypothesis.
- 2026-06-06 adjacent endpoint extraction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTight_contour_interval_sameCycle_exists_adjacent_left_repeat`
  converts the endpoint-touching repeat into a genuinely adjacent endpoint link
  once non-adjacent endpoint-touching repeats are also excluded.  This closes
  the local arithmetic extraction after the minimal-interval branch.  The active
  theorem-strength leaf remains `leftTight_bianeAdjacentLinks`: the global
  proof still has to produce the required exclusion hypotheses from an arbitrary
  genuine crossing.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart singleton reconstruction:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.isBlockMin_and_isBlockMax_iff_part_singleton` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_singleton_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` prove that
  singleton blocks are exactly the points whose min/max marker code records
  both endpoint flags, and that this singleton status is preserved by equal
  min/max codes.  This advances the `NCPart.minMaxCode` cardinality route; full
  min/max-code injectivity is still open.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart interval-containment rule:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_Icc_of_between_distinct` packages the
  noncrossing nesting obstruction in the form needed by a first-return proof:
  if one block spans `i<j` and a distinct block meets the open interval at
  `k`, then the whole `k`-block is contained in `[i,j]`.  This is a structural
  count-side lemma toward reconstructing noncrossing partitions from their
  min/max marker code; it does not by itself prove min/max-code injectivity.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart endpoint-containment rule:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_Icc_of_between_endpoints` upgrades the
  previous containment lemma to the endpoint-marker formulation: when `i` is
  the minimum marker and `j` the maximum marker of a block, every block meeting
  the open interval `(i,j)` is contained in `[i,j]`.  This is the exact nesting
  fact needed by first-block/first-return reconstruction from min/max codes.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart initial-block containment:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zero_isBlockMin` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_initial_interval_of_lt_zeroBlockMax` specialize
  endpoint containment to the block containing `0`: once its maximum marker `j`
  is known, every block represented before `j` is contained in the initial
  interval ending at `j`.  This prepares the first-return split for the
  min/max-code injectivity route.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart closed initial-block markers:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_initial_interval_of_le_zeroBlockMax` extends
  the initial-block containment through the endpoint `j`, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_min_max_markers_le_of_le_zeroBlockMax` shows that
  the min/max markers of every block represented at `k ≤ j` also lie before
  or at `j`.  This gives the marker-level ingredient needed for the first-block
  recursion behind `NCPart.minMaxCode` injectivity; the full injectivity and
  genus-zero count remain open.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart first-return imbalance:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixMinMarker`,
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixMaxMarker`, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixMaxMarker_card_lt_prefixMinMarker_card_of_lt_zeroBlockMax`
  formalize the key prefix fact for the zero block.  Before the maximum marker
  `j` of the block containing `0`, the number of closed blocks in the prefix is
  strictly smaller than the number of opened blocks.  The checked proof uses
  the canonical block-minimum marker map from prefix maxima to prefix minima
  and proves that the marker `0` is outside its image.  This is the first
  checked first-return inequality for the min/max-code reconstruction route.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart prefix-code transport:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixMinMarker_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixMaxMarker_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` transport prefix
  endpoint-marker predicates across equal min/max codes, and the card-count
  versions `...prefixMinMarker_card_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq` and
  `...prefixMaxMarker_card_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq` prove equality of the prefix
  counts.  Combined with the prefix imbalance theorem, the zero-block
  first-return candidate is now a code-visible quantity; the remaining step is
  to prove it is exactly the zero-block maximum.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart first-return balance:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.blockMaxMarker` adds the canonical maximum marker of a
  block, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixMinMarker_card_eq_prefixMaxMarker_card_of_zeroBlockMax`
  proves that the prefix ending at the maximum marker `j` of the block
  containing `0` has equal numbers of minimum and maximum markers.  Together
  with the strict prefix imbalance before `j`, this gives the checked
  first-return pattern needed to recover the first block from the min/max code.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart zero-block close recovery:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixBalanced` names the balanced-prefix predicate,
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixBalanced_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` transports it across
  equal min/max codes, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockMax_val_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq` proves that equal
  min/max codes force the same maximum-marker value for the block containing
  `0`.  This closes the first-return value-recovery step; the remaining
  reconstruction work is recursive transport of the interval subpartitions.
- 2026-06-06 NCPart zero-block predicate transport:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockMax_mem_and_isBlockMax_of_minMaxCode_eq` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockMax_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` upgrade the close
  value recovery into the actual predicate needed downstream: equal min/max
  codes preserve the statement that a given point is the maximum marker of the
  block containing `0`.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart suffix split:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_suffix_interval_of_zeroBlockMax` proves that
  once the block containing `0` closes at `j`, every block represented after
  `j` lies strictly after `j`; the proof is the direct noncrossing obstruction
  against an interleaving `0 < x < j < k`.  The marker version
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_min_max_markers_gt_of_gt_zeroBlockMax` gives the
  same suffix containment for block endpoints.  This supplies the suffix half
  of the recursive min/max-code split.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart canonical zero-block split API:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockMaxMarker` names the zero-block close, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockMaxMarker_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq` proves equal
  min/max codes have the same close.  The specialized initial/suffix lemmas
  with suffixes `...zeroBlockMaxMarker` package the recursive split without
  carrying explicit close-witness hypotheses.  This prepares the induced
  subpartition recursion for `NCPart.minMaxCode` injectivity.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart prefix-excess scanner:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixExcess` formalizes opened-minus-closed block count
  in a prefix.  It is code-visible
  (`NCPartition.NCPart.prefixExcess_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq`), nonnegative via the
  prefix max-to-min injection
  (`NCPartition.NCPart.prefixMaxMarker_card_le_prefixMinMarker_card`), and the
  zero-block first-return facts are now stated in this language:
  `...prefixExcess_eq_zero_of_zeroBlockMax` and
  `...prefixExcess_pos_of_lt_zeroBlockMax`.  This is the scanner invariant for
  recursive code reconstruction.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart first-zero scanner:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixExcessFirstZero` packages the first return of the
  prefix-excess scanner to zero.  It is transported by equal min/max codes,
  unique, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockMaxMarker_prefixExcessFirstZero` identifies the
  canonical zero-block close with that first zero.  This gives the recursive
  split a code-visible first-return definition rather than a witnessed
  partition fact.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block code-member candidate:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockCodeMember` names the code-visible candidate for
  membership in the block containing `0`: the endpoint `0`, the recovered close,
  or a neutral point at prefix-excess height one before the close.
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockCodeMember_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` proves this
  candidate transports across equal min/max codes.  The correctness theorem
  equating the candidate with actual zero-block membership is still open.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block crossing support:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.block_element_le_of_blockMin_le_mem_zeroBlock` proves
  that a nonzero block opened at or before a point of the zero block cannot
  extend past that point.  This is the noncrossing crossing obstruction needed
  for the forward correctness direction of `zeroBlockCodeMember`.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block endpoint/neutral support:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.lt_zeroBlockMaxMarker_of_mem_zeroBlock_of_ne_zeroBlockMaxMarker`
  proves zero-block points other than the close lie before it, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.isBlockNeutral_of_mem_zeroBlock_of_ne_zero_of_ne_zeroBlockMaxMarker`
  proves zero-block interior points are neutral.  Together with the crossing
  support lemma, this narrows the forward correctness proof to the remaining
  prefix-excess-height-one count.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block forward correctness:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.prefixExcess_eq_one_of_mem_zeroBlock_lt_zeroBlockMax`
  proves actual zero-block interior points have prefix-excess height one, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockCodeMember_of_mem_zeroBlock` closes the forward
  direction from actual zero-block membership to the code-side candidate.  The
  reverse direction is the remaining local reconstruction theorem.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block membership reconstruction:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.two_le_prefixExcess_of_not_mem_zeroBlock_of_lt_zeroBlockMaxMarker_of_neutral`
  proves the reverse-direction obstruction: a neutral candidate outside the
  zero block would force prefix-excess at least two.  Therefore
  `NCPartition.NCPart.mem_zeroBlock_of_zeroBlockCodeMember` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockCodeMember_iff_mem_zeroBlock` reconstruct the
  block containing `0` from the min/max code.  The next step is recursive
  subpartition reconstruction.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block set transport:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlock_part_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq` proves that equal
  min/max codes give equal actual blocks containing `0`.  This packages the
  recovered membership predicate into the set-level equality needed before the
  recursive initial/suffix subpartition reconstruction step.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block block transport:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_eq_of_mem_zeroBlock_of_minMaxCode_eq` upgrades the
  set-level zero-block transport to full block equality for any point in that
  recovered block.  This is the local partition-extensionality bridge before
  proving the recursive initial/suffix reconstruction.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart reconstruction endpoint:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.eq_of_forall_part_eq` proves that pointwise equality of
  the block containing each point determines a noncrossing partition, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.minMaxCode_injective_of_forall_part_eq` reduces the
  min/max-code injectivity theorem to exactly that pointwise block
  reconstruction under equal codes.  The zero-block cases are now supplied by
  the previous block-transport lemma; the open branch is recursive recovery of
  nonzero initial/suffix blocks.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block pointwise case:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_eq_of_zeroBlockCodeMember_of_minMaxCode_eq` exposes
  the zero-block case in the same shape as the pointwise reconstruction target:
  a code-detected zero-block point has the same full block under equal min/max
  codes.  The remaining pointwise cases are the nonzero initial and suffix
  recursive subpartitions.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart nonzero recursive split:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.ne_zeroBlockMaxMarker_of_not_mem_zeroBlock` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.lt_or_gt_zeroBlockMaxMarker_of_not_mem_zeroBlock` prove
  that every point outside the zero block lies strictly on one side of the
  recovered close.  This supplies the case split for the pending induced
  initial/suffix subpartition recursion.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart open initial recursion:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_open_initial_interval_of_not_mem_zeroBlock_of_lt_zeroBlockMaxMarker`
  proves that a nonzero block represented before the zero-block close is
  contained strictly inside the open initial interval.  The companion marker
  lemma `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_min_max_markers_open_initial_of_not_mem_zeroBlock_of_lt_zeroBlockMaxMarker`
  puts both endpoint markers inside that interval.  This removes endpoint
  leakage from the future induced initial subpartition.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart recursive-region transport:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.not_mem_zeroBlock_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq`,
  `NCPartition.NCPart.lt_zeroBlockMaxMarker_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq`, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockMaxMarker_lt_iff_of_minMaxCode_eq` transport the
  complement of the zero block and both sides of the recovered close across
  equal min/max codes.  The block containment lemmas
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_open_initial_interval_of_minMaxCode_eq` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_suffix_interval_of_minMaxCode_eq` apply the
  initial/suffix split to the other equal-code partition.  The remaining step is
  still the actual induced-subpartition equality/recursion.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart branch reduction:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.forall_part_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq_of_initial_suffix` proves
  that full pointwise block reconstruction follows from the two nonzero
  recursive branches: open-initial blocks and suffix blocks.  The theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.minMaxCode_injective_of_initial_suffix_reconstruction`
  then turns those branch suppliers into `NCPart.minMaxCode` injectivity.  This
  makes the exact remaining count-side local leaf the induced-subpartition
  recursion for those two branches.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart zero-block gap confinement:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_open_interval_of_between_zeroBlock_points`
  proves the genuine multi-gap recursion fact: whenever two zero-block points
  bracket a nonzero-block representative, that representative's whole block
  stays strictly inside the bracketed gap.  The companion marker theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_min_max_markers_open_interval_of_between_zeroBlock_points`
  keeps the block endpoints in the same gap.  This corrects the simplified
  initial/suffix picture by accounting for interior points of the zero block.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart transported gap confinement:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_open_interval_of_between_zeroBlock_points_of_minMaxCode_eq`
  transports the zero-block gap confinement to any equal-code partition, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_min_max_markers_open_interval_of_between_zeroBlock_points_of_minMaxCode_eq`
  does the same for min/max markers.  Equal min/max codes therefore preserve
  not only the zero-block set but also the recursive gap containers cut out by
  consecutive zero-block points.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart nearest gap brackets:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_zeroBlock_gap_bounds_of_not_mem_zeroBlock_of_lt_zeroBlockMaxMarker`
  constructs, for a nonzero point before the zero-block close, the last
  zero-block point on its left and the first zero-block point on its right.
  The theorem proves the bracketing inequalities, max/min universal
  properties, and absence of any zero-block point strictly inside the selected
  gap.  This provides the concrete gap boundaries needed for induced
  subpartition recursion.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart common gap-bounds API:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockGapBounds` packages the consecutive-gap data as
  a reusable predicate.  The checked transport theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockGapBounds_of_minMaxCode_eq` proves that equal
  min/max codes preserve the same gap bounds, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_common_zeroBlockGapBounds_of_minMaxCode_eq`
  constructs common bounds for both equal-code partitions around the same
  nonzero point.  This is the interface the induced gap-recursion proof should
  consume.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart gap-bound confinement API:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.not_mem_zeroBlock_of_zeroBlockGapBounds` proves that the
  representative in a reusable gap-bound package is not in the zero block.
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_open_interval_of_zeroBlockGapBounds` then
  confines its whole block to the selected gap, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_open_interval_of_zeroBlockGapBounds_of_minMaxCode_eq`
  gives the same confinement for the matching block in an equal-code
  partition.  The induced gap-recursion proof can now consume the packaged
  bounds directly.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart gap-bound marker API:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_min_max_markers_open_interval_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  upgrades the packaged gap bounds from block confinement to min/max marker
  confinement.  The transported theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.exists_min_max_markers_open_interval_of_zeroBlockGapBounds_of_minMaxCode_eq`
  gives the same marker data for an equal-code partition.  This keeps the
  induced gap-subpartition recursion focused on constructing the restriction,
  rather than repeatedly unpacking noncrossing containment.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart gap/suffix count reduction:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.initial_reconstruction_of_zeroBlockGap_reconstruction`
  proves that the open-initial branch follows from reconstruction on each
  consecutive zero-block gap.  The theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.minMaxCode_injective_of_gap_suffix_reconstruction`
  reduces min/max-code injectivity to gap-local reconstruction plus suffix
  reconstruction, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.card_NCPart_succ_le_four_pow_of_gap_suffix_reconstruction`
  is the count-facing version.  This narrows the `NCPart` cardinal leaf to the
  induced gap-subpartition and suffix-recursion constructions.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart induced-gap carrier start:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapLength` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapCarrier` name the smaller interval
  carrier used by the induced gap recursion.  The checked facts
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapLength_pos_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`,
  `NCPartition.NCPart.nonempty_zeroBlockOpenGapCarrier_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`,
  and `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapCarrierOfMemPart` connect a packaged
  zero-block gap to that carrier.  The induced partition itself is still the
  next theorem-strength construction.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart raw gap finpartition:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapFinset` names the open gap as a finset of
  ambient labels, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapFinpartition` restricts an ambient
  partition to that gap with mathlib's `Finpartition.restrict`.  The theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.mem_zeroBlockOpenGapFinset_of_mem_part_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  proves that every element of a gap-confined block lands in this restricted
  carrier.  The next construction is the order reindexing of this raw
  restriction to an `NCPart` on `Fin (zeroBlockOpenGapLength i j)`.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart open-gap reindexing:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapFinEquiv` identifies the open ambient gap
  with `Fin (zeroBlockOpenGapLength i j)`, with checked coordinate formulas in
  both directions.  The bridge
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapCarrierEquivFinsetSubtype` relates this
  carrier to the raw open-gap finset, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.card_zeroBlockOpenGapFinset` proves that the raw gap has
  exactly the intrinsic length.  This is the coordinate layer needed before
  transporting `zeroBlockOpenGapFinpartition` to a smaller `NCPart`.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart raw gap encode/decode:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapEncode` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapDecode` name the arithmetic maps between
  ambient labels and gap coordinates.  The checked membership lemmas prove that
  they exchange `zeroBlockOpenGapFinset i j` with
  `range (zeroBlockOpenGapLength i j)`, and the round-trip lemmas prove the
  coordinate identities.  These are the raw ingredients for mapping each
  restricted partition part to the intrinsic gap coordinates.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart coordinate images of gap parts:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapMapFinset` maps an ambient finset through
  the open-gap encoder.  The theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapMapFinset_subset_range` keeps such images
  inside the intrinsic coordinate range when the source lies in the open gap,
  and `NCPartition.NCPart.mem_zeroBlockOpenGapMapFinset_iff_of_subset` gives
  the decoded membership criterion.  This is the part-level API needed to build
  the induced coordinate finpartition with `Finpartition.ofExistsUnique`.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart candidate induced-gap parts:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapReindexedParts` maps the raw restricted
  partition parts into intrinsic gap coordinates.  The checked obligations
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapReindexedParts_subset_range` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.empty_notMem_zeroBlockOpenGapReindexedParts` prove,
  respectively, that every candidate part lies in the coordinate range and no
  candidate part is empty.  The remaining `Finpartition.ofExistsUnique`
  constructor leaf is the unique-cover theorem for these mapped parts.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart induced gap finpartition:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.existsUnique_mem_zeroBlockOpenGapReindexedParts` proves
  the unique-cover theorem for the mapped gap parts, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapReindexedFinpartition` builds the induced
  coordinate finpartition on `range (zeroBlockOpenGapLength i j)`.  The part
  API
  `NCPartition.NCPart.mem_zeroBlockOpenGapReindexedFinpartition_part_iff`
  identifies membership in an induced coordinate block with decoded membership
  in the raw restricted open-gap block.  The next step is packaging this
  finpartition as an `NCPart` and proving min/max-code inheritance.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart induced gap `NCPart`:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapFinpartition_part_eq_inter` proves the
  raw restricted part of an open-gap point is the ambient part intersected with
  the open-gap finset.  Using this bridge,
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapReindexedFinpartition_nonCrossing`
  proves the reindexed gap finpartition is noncrossing, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapNCPart` packages it as an actual
  `NCPart (zeroBlockOpenGapLength i j)`.  The remaining induced-recursion work
  is min/max-code inheritance and then the smaller-`NCPart` induction lift.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart gap-bound ambient bridge:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_subset_open_interval_of_mem_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  proves that, once `i,j` are consecutive zero-block bounds, any ambient block
  represented inside `(i,j)` stays inside `(i,j)`.  Therefore
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapFinpartition_part_eq_ambient_part_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  identifies raw restricted parts with full ambient parts for points in the
  gap.  This is the key hypothesis-specific bridge needed for min/max-code
  inheritance on `zeroBlockOpenGapNCPart`.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart induced gap code inheritance:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapNCPart_isBlockMin_iff_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapNCPart_isBlockMax_iff_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  prove that induced gap min/max marker predicates are exactly the ambient
  predicates restricted to the gap.  The pointwise theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapNCPart_minMaxCode_apply_eq_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  and the transported theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockOpenGapNCPart_minMaxCode_eq_of_minMaxCode_eq`
  give the min/max-code equality needed by the smaller-`NCPart` induction
  lift.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart initial branch via smaller gaps:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.part_eq_of_zeroBlockOpenGapNCPart_eq_of_zeroBlockGapBounds`
  lifts equality of induced gap partitions back to equality of the ambient
  block represented in the gap.  The theorem
  `NCPartition.NCPart.zeroBlockGap_reconstruction_of_smaller_minMaxCode_injective`
  uses smaller-`NCPart` min/max-code injectivity to reconstruct each gap block,
  and `NCPartition.NCPart.initial_reconstruction_of_smaller_minMaxCode_injective`
  supplies the whole open-initial branch.  The recursive `NCPart` cardinal
  route is now narrowed to the suffix branch after the zero-block close.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart suffix coordinate layer:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixLength`, `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixFinset`, and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixCarrier` name the suffix strictly after an ambient
  point.  `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixFinEquiv` identifies it with
  `Fin (suffixLength j)`, and the encode/decode lemmas prove the membership
  and round-trip facts needed to build the induced suffix partition.  This is
  the suffix analogue of the earlier open-gap coordinate layer.
- 2026-06-07 NCPart induced suffix `NCPart`:
  `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixFinpartition` restricts an ambient partition to the
  suffix, while `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixReindexedParts` and
  `NCPartition.NCPart.existsUnique_mem_suffixReindexedParts` build the
  coordinate finpartition.  `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixReindexedFinpartition_nonCrossing`
  proves that finpartition is noncrossing, and `NCPartition.NCPart.suffixNCPart`
  packages it as an actual `NCPart (suffixLength j)`.  The remaining suffix
  branch work is min/max-code inheritance and the smaller-`NCPart` lift.
- 2026-06-06 partition-injectivity transport bridge:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTightCyclePartitionRange_eq_iff_sameCycle_setoid_eq`
  proves that equality of the transported cycle partitions on
  `Finset.range (m+1)` is exactly equality of the underlying `π` same-cycle
  setoids.  This removes the `Fin.val` transport from the injectivity leaf:
  what remains is the genuine reconstruction theorem for left-tight
  permutations with the same cycle setoid.
- 2026-06-06 ordered-predecessor reconstruction reduction:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.setoidBlockCyclicPred` formalizes the candidate
  reconstruction rule seen in finite left-tight cases: `π x` is the cyclic
  predecessor of `x` inside its cycle block in natural order.  The theorem
  `setoidBlockCyclicPred_unique` proves this predecessor is unique, and
  `leftTightCyclePartitionRange_injective_of_orderedPredecessorLaw` proves
  the ordered-predecessor law implies injectivity of the left-tight partition
  encoding.  The adapter
  `wickPlusGenusCount_zero_le_four_pow_of_NCPart_count_orderedPredecessor`
  replaces the opaque injectivity input by this concrete reconstruction law.
- 2026-06-06 order-only predecessor leaf:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTightOrderedPredecessorOrderLaw` removes the
  automatic same-cycle conjunct from the reconstruction leaf: `π x` is always
  in the same `π`-cycle as `x`, so the remaining injectivity input is only the
  cyclic-order maximality of `π x` inside its block.  The checked adapter
  `leftTightOrderedPredecessorLaw_of_orderLaw` feeds this order-only law back
  into the existing injectivity reduction.
- 2026-06-06 fixed-point predecessor case:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.leftTightOrderedPredecessorOrderLaw_at_fixed`
  proves the order-only predecessor condition at every fixed point `π x = x`.
  This closes the singleton-cycle branch of the reconstruction problem; the
  non-fixed cycle case remains the theorem-strength injectivity leaf.
- 2026-06-06 length-four count closure checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.WickCyclePairTightClassFormula` is a computable
  cycle-type version of the simultaneous-geodesic class, and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_WickCyclePairTightClass_eq_formula`
  proves it has the same cardinality as `WickCyclePairTightClass`.
  The kernel-checked finite theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.card_WickCyclePairTightClass_four_le_32`
  closes the `m=4`, zero-defect slice; positive defects are handled by
  the crude factorial count and the defect-power factor.  The checked endpoint
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPermutationDefectCount_four_tightRange_le_fixedEnvelope`
  now closes all tight-range defects for `m=4`.  This is still a finite
  small-length closure and does not replace the general innovation-fiber
  package required by `U-AUB-02`.
- 2026-06-06 length-five positive-defect checkpoint:
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPermutationDefectCount_five_posDefect_tightRange_le_fixedEnvelope`
  closes every positive tight-range defect slice for `m=5` by combining the
  crude `|S₆|=720` count with the defect-power factor.  The checked reduction
  theorem
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.wickPermutationDefectCount_five_tightRange_le_fixedEnvelope_of_cyclePairTightCount`
  reduces the whole `m=5` tight-range count estimate to the single finite
  zero-defect input `Fintype.card (WickCyclePairTightClass 5) ≤ 64`.
  The module build passed, and the new pair of theorems audits to the usual
  Lean/mathlib foundations `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.
- Sharpest checked public endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_canonical_Qone`.
- Public compressed-scalar endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_tightCountCrudeCommonBase_canonical_Qone`.
- Public cancelled-scalar endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_tightCountCrudeCommonBase_cancelTwo_canonical_Qone`.
- Public absorbed-polynomial scalar endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_absorbPCommonBase_cancelTwo_canonical_Qone`.
- Public absorbed-`Q` common-base scalar endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_absorbQCommonBase_cancelTwo_canonical_Qone`.
- Public finite-ratio scalar endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_ratioCommonBase_cancelTwo_canonical_Qone`.
- Public geometric-ratio scalar endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_geometricRatioCommonBase_cancelTwo_canonical_Qone`.
- Public absorbed-`Q` margin endpoint:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_support_cayley_rank_absorbQCommonBase_cancelTwo_margins_canonical_Qone`.
- Absorbed-`Q` margin no-go diagnostic:
  `AppendixB.aubrun_absorbedQ_commonBaseMargins_impossible_of_one_le_m`.
- Public absorbed-`Q` margin no-go diagnostic:
  `AppendixB.aubrun_absorbedQ_commonBaseMargins_impossible_for_evenMoment`.
- Checked graduate supplier for that endpoint:
  `AppendixB.aubrunGraduateRelationCounting_of_prop73FiberDataSupportAndCayleyRankBudget`.
- Actual defect support:
  `AppendixB.wickDefectSupport`.
- Tight support range:
  `AppendixB.wickDefectSupport_subset_tightRange`, supplied by
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.four_le_wickWeightedRank` and
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickDefect_le_two_mul`.
- Support count-envelope sum:
  `AppendixB.aubrunFixedDefectCountEnvelope_support_sum_le_card_mul_tightLast`.
- Tight-count crude common-base scalar wrapper:
  `AppendixB.aubrunGraduateRelationCounting_of_prop73FiberDataSupportAndCayleyRankBudget_tightCountCrudeCommonBase`.
- Support-sum bridge:
  `AppendixB.aubrunGraduateRelationCounting_of_defectSupportSumBound`.
- Count-to-fiber bridge:
  `AppendixB.wickPermutationDefectCount_real_le_fixedEnvelope_of_embedding_to_sigma`.
- Range-based fiber endpoint, still available as an intermediate adapter:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_fiber_data_cayley_rank_canonical_Qone`.
- Count-only Cayley endpoint, still available as an intermediate adapter:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_prop73_cayley_rank_canonical_Qone`.
- Crude-route diagnostic:
  `AppendixB.crudeLastEnvelope_degree_obstruction_m_one`.
- Defect-sensitive algebra bridge:
  `AppendixB.wickAnalyticWeight_commonBase_eq_defectPower`.
- Two-base analytic bridge:
  `AppendixB.wickAnalyticWeight_le_commonBase_defectPower`.
- Pure combinatorial edge bridge:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickLeftRelGen_iff_perm_edge`,
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickRightRelGen_iff_perm_edge`,
  and `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickColumnRelGen_iff_perm_edge`.
- Cycle-relation bridge:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.permEdgeRel_eqvGen_iff_sameCycle`.
- Cycle-count bridge:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickLeftClassCount_eq_cycleClassCount`,
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickRightClassCount_eq_cycleClassCount`,
  and
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickColumnClassCount_eq_cycleClassCount`.
- Rank-budget rewrite:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickWeightedRank_eq_cycleClassBudget`.
- Full-cycle connectedness:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.finRotate_sameCycle_all_of_two_le`
  and
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.permCycleClassCount_finRotate_of_pos`.
- Cycle-count normalization:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.permCycleClassCount_inv`,
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.permCycleClassCount_conj`,
  and
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.permCycleClassCount_mul_comm`.
- Cycle-pair rank adapter:
