  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickWeightedRank_le_of_cyclePairBounds`.
- Inverse-long-cycle convention bridge:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.cyclePairLeftBound_of_inverseLongCycleInvBound`,
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.cyclePairRightBound_of_inverseLongCycleBound`,
  and
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickWeightedRank_le_of_inverseLongCycleBounds`.
- Closed Cayley rank budget:
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.setoidQuotientCard_add_le_card_add_one_of_sup_top`,
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.cyclePair_inverseLongCycleBound`,
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.cyclePair_inverseLongCycleInvBound`,
  and
  `TraceWickExpansion.AubrunSurvivingCounting.wickWeightedRank_le_two_mul_add_two`.
- Challenge Before:
  the public crude Aubrun pipeline exposed per-defect fiber data but still used
  a maximum-exponent scalar route.  That route is now diagnosed as the wrong
  final closure mechanism by a degree obstruction.
- Challenge After:
  the public pipeline exposes the sharp defect-sensitive route over actual
  defect support only: support-indexed Aubrun fiber data, the closed
  weighted-rank budget, and one support-restricted scalar defect-sum envelope.
  The fixed-defect count hypothesis has been replaced by explicit embeddings
  into innovation fibers, compatible-couple bounds, and the large-innovation
  inequality, and impossible defect values no longer appear in the scalar
  frontier.  The top four coarse-range defect values are also removed: every
  actual defect satisfies `Δ ≤ 2m`, because each of the left, right, and column
  quotient class counts is nonzero and therefore `wickWeightedRank π ≥ 4`.
  Consequently, the actual-support sum of the fixed-defect count envelopes is
  checked with the tighter top exponent `2m` and support-card factor `2m+1`:
  `Σ_{Δ∈support} 2^k P(k)^Δ ≤ (2m+1) 2^k P(k)^(2m)`.
  This has also been fed into a crude common-base scalar wrapper:
  for `1 ≤ D`,
  `Σ_{Δ∈support} 2^k P(k)^Δ D^(2k+2-Δ) ≤
  (2m+1)2^kP(k)^(2m)D^(2k+2)`.  This is a useful scalar adapter, not the final
  large-scale scalar comparison.  The same compressed scalar input is now also
  exposed at the public expectation-pipeline layer, so the public Aubrun
  frontier can consume support-indexed fiber data plus one displayed scalar
  inequality directly.  The common `2^k` factor has also been cancelled at the
  graduate and public layers, leaving the cleaner scalar leaf
  `(2m+1)P(k)^(2m)D^(2k+2) ≤ (d+Q(k))^(m+3)(sqrt(s)+Q(k))^k`.
  A stronger support-sum adapter now absorbs the fixed-defect polynomial factor
  into the common base: if `P(k) ≤ D`, then
  `Σ_{Δ∈support} 2^kP(k)^ΔD^(2k+2-Δ) ≤ (2m+1)2^kD^(2k+2)`.
  Thus the sharpest public scalar leaf is
  `(2m+1)D^(2k+2) ≤ (d+Q(k))^(m+3)(sqrt(s)+Q(k))^k`, plus the side condition
  `P(k) ≤ D`.  The side condition has now been sharpened to the natural common
  base condition `Q(k) ≤ D` for the canonical choice `Q(k)=kP(k)`: since
  `k≥1`, `Q(k)≤D` implies `P(k)≤D`.  The sharpest public scalar endpoint now
  assumes that `D` bounds `d`, `sqrt(s)`, and `Q(k)`, and asks for the single
  scalar comparison
  `(2m+1)D^(2k+2) ≤ (d+Q(k))^(m+3)(sqrt(s)+Q(k))^k`.
  That displayed scalar comparison can also be supplied by two simpler margin
  inequalities:
  `D ≤ d+Q(k)` and `(2m+1)D ≤ sqrt(s)+Q(k)`.  This isolates the remaining
  support-card factor as a base-margin/asymptotic obligation rather than a
  monolithic scalar leaf.  However, that margin split is now checked to be a
  dead final common-base route: if the same common base also satisfies
  `sqrt(s)≤D` and `Q(k)≤D`, then the second margin would force
  `(2m+1)D≤2D`, impossible for `m≥1` and `D>0`.  The public endpoint version
  is checked too, since `aubrunEvenMomentParameter d - 1 ≥ 1`.
  The analytic term side is checked down to the rank-budget input:
  if both `d` and `sqrt s` are bounded by a common nonnegative base `D`, a
  defect-`Δ` Wick term is bounded by `D^(2k+2-Δ)`.  The rank-budget proof has
  also been moved into a standard permutation language: the left/right/column
  quotient class counts are now the cycle counts of `finRotate*π`,
  `π*finRotate⁻¹`, and `π`, and `wickWeightedRank π` is exactly the
  corresponding three-cycle-count budget.  The full finite rotation is checked
  to have one cycle class; cycle counts are also normalized under inverse,
  conjugation, and product-order swap.  The usual mathematical form using
  `π⁻¹γ` and `π⁻¹γ⁻¹` maps to the repository convention by checked adapters.
  The two Cayley inequalities are now proved from the finite incidence-graph
  partition inequality, so the public endpoint no longer consumes any rank,
  cycle-count, fixed-defect-count, or impossible-defect hypothesis.
- Status:
  this is checked adapter/diagnostic progress, not a closure of `U-AUB-02`.
  The theorem still needs the actual support-indexed fiber construction and the
  support-restricted scalar defect-sum estimate.  The weighted-rank budget,
  count-to-fiber adapter, defect-support restriction, and tight support range
  are closed; the support count-envelope sum has also been tightened and wired
  into a crude common-base scalar wrapper at both the graduate-counting and
  public expectation-pipeline layers, with the common `2^k` cancelled in the
  intermediate displayed scalar endpoint.  The sharpest displayed scalar
  endpoint also absorbs the fixed-defect polynomial factor into `D` under the
  side condition `Q(k) ≤ D`.  The later margin endpoint is retained only as a
  checked diagnostic: under the common-base assumptions it cannot close the
  asymptotic scalar comparison, and the public logarithmic even-moment
  specialization has `m≥1` automatically.  The live scalar route therefore
  returns to a defect-sensitive finite ratio sum: under `P(k)≤ρD`, the
  support-restricted scalar side is bounded by
  `2^k D^(2k+2) Σ_{Δ∈support}ρ^Δ`, so the public endpoint now asks for the
  displayed finite-ratio comparison rather than a support-card margin.  A
  further checked adapter bounds this finite sum by the geometric-series
  constant `(1-ρ)⁻¹` whenever `0≤ρ<1`, giving the sharper public scalar leaf
  `(1-ρ)⁻¹D^(2k+2) ≤ (d+Q(k))^(m+3)(sqrt(s)+Q(k))^k`.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBPipelineGraduate` and
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBAubrunGraduate` pass.  The sharp
  support/fiber endpoint, graduate supplier, support-sum bridge, tight support
  range, support count-envelope sum, tight-count crude scalar wrappers at the
  graduate and public pipeline layers, cancelled-`2^k` scalar wrappers, scalar
  diagnostic, absorbed-polynomial, absorbed-`Q` common-base wrappers,
  absorbed-`Q` margin wrappers, the absorbed-`Q` margin no-go diagnostic, its
  public even-moment form, finite-ratio scalar wrappers, and geometric-ratio
  scalar wrappers, defect-power bridges, class-count cycle bridges,
  weighted-rank cycle-budget rewrite, full-rotation cycle-count helper,
  cycle-count normalization lemmas, and closed Cayley rank-budget theorem audit to
  `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

## 2026-06-05 Word-Protocol Local Spike Module

- New module:
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/WordProtocolLocalSpike.lean`.
- Status:
  WP-00 project placement and WP-01 word API are checked.  The file introduces
  the alphabet \(A,P,C\), word counts, pure/mixed predicates, and the delicate
  \(P,C\) branch predicate.
- First checked reduction endpoint:
  `PptFactorization.WordProtocol.upperTailLocalInput_of_wordProtocol`.
  It states that a word-protocol certificate plus concrete assembly rules
  supplies the local input for the upper-tail pipeline.
- Important limitation:
  the module is an abstract reduction layer.  It does not prove the concrete
  matrix decomposition, finite trace expansion, pure-term estimates, or mixed
  structural estimates.  Those remain explicit fields of
  `WordProtocolRules`/`WordProtocolCertificate`, with the \(P,C\) structural
  branch still the main hard leaf.
- Verification:
  `lake env lean PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/WordProtocolLocalSpike.lean`
  and `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.WordProtocolLocalSpike`
  pass.  The required forbidden-token scan on the new file is clean.  Dependency
  inspection of the two public reduction endpoints reports only `propext`.

## 2026-06-05 Word-Protocol Expansion Progress

- WP-03 abstract finite expansion is checked:
  `PptFactorization.WordProtocol.Word.exact_finite_word_expansion`.
  For any semiring and noncommuting terms \(A,P,C\), it proves
  \[
    \sum_{w\in\{A,P,C\}^n}\operatorname{eval}(w)=(A+P+C)^n
  \]
  using recursively generated words and ordered products.
- WP-04 scalar coefficient formula is checked:
  `PptFactorization.WordProtocol.Word.evalProduct_coeff_eq_beta`.
  In a commutative coefficient monoid, the ordered scalar coefficient product
  equals \(a^{\#A(w)}p^{\#P(w)}c^{\#C(w)}\).
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.WordProtocolLocalSpike` passes;
  forbidden-token scan on the file is clean.  Dependency inspection of both new
  theorems reports only `propext` and `Quot.sound`.
- Remaining caveat:
  this closes the abstract algebra and scalar coefficient layer, not the
  concrete matrix trace expansion.  The next concrete ticket is to connect this
  word expansion to the project matrix/trace expression for
  \(\operatorname{Tr}((aA+bP+cC)^k)\).

## 2026-06-05 Word-Protocol Local-Expansion Bridge

- New module:
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/WordProtocolLocalSpikeBridge.lean`.
- WP-05 adapter bridge is checked:
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.isMixed_protocolWordOfLocal_iff`.
  It connects the manuscript protocol alphabet \(A,P,C\) to the compiled
  local-expansion alphabet \(A,L,Q\) by the dictionary
  \[
    A\mapsto A,\qquad P\mapsto Q,\qquad C\mapsto L.
  \]
  After this renaming, the protocol mixed predicate is exactly the project
  local-expansion mixed predicate.
- Supporting checked facts:
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.countA_protocolWordOfLocal`,
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.countP_protocolWordOfLocal`, and
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.countC_protocolWordOfLocal` identify protocol
  counts with the corresponding local letter counts.  The pure-word/count
  equivalence
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.localWordIsPure_iff_letterCount_eq` supplies
  the finite-word bookkeeping.
- WP-06 product bridge is checked:
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.evalProduct_protocolWordOfLocal_eq_localWordMatrixProduct`.
  For every concrete local-expansion word \(w\),
  \[
    \operatorname{eval}_{A,P,C}(\Phi(w); A,Q,L)
      = \operatorname{localProduct}_{A,L,Q}(w).
  \]
  This connects the abstract ordered word product to the concrete matrix word
  product used in the local trace expansion.
- WP-07 local branch assembly is checked:
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.localWordScaledTraceTerm_le_envelope_of_branchNormBounds`.
  It packages the three deterministic mixed-word branches into one local
  envelope statement.  Under the pointwise bounds
  \[
    \|A\|_{\mathrm{op}}\le M/N,\qquad
    \|L\|_2\le L_1/\sqrt{|p\times q|},\qquad
    \|L\|_2\le L_2,\qquad
    \|Q\|_2\le Q_1/\sqrt{|p\times q|},
  \]
  every mixed word \(w\) satisfies
  \[
    \left|\mathrm{scaledTrace}_N(A,L,Q;w)\right|
      \le
    \mathrm{EnvelopeTerm}_N(A_{\mathrm{bd}},L_2,L_1,Q_2,Q_1;k,w).
  \]
  The proof is exactly the three-way split: one linear defect, one quadratic
  defect, or the remaining multi-defect case.
- WP-08 concrete trace bridge is checked:
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.localWordScaledTraceTerm_eq_protocol_evalProduct_trace`,
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.scaledTracePower_eq_sum_protocolWordOfLocalScaledTraceTerm`,
  and
  `AppendixB.WordProtocolBridge.localExpansionMixedRemainder_eq_sum_protocolMixedWordScaledTraceTerm`.
  These state that, after the dictionary
  \(A\mapsto A,\ P\mapsto Q,\ C\mapsto L\), the protocol products are exactly
  the concrete normalized trace words, and the concrete mixed remainder is the
  filtered protocol trace-word sum.

  this closes the adapter, concrete trace-expansion, and local branch-assembly

  bounds, nor does it close the scalar smallness limits for the chosen
  one-quadratic strategy.  Those remain live theorem-strength inputs.

  the lower mixed input is stated at the right level: prove that a common
  \(o(1)\) lower mixed error frontier exists; its chosen formula is internal
  bookkeeping.

- The finite-diagram length-three upper endpoint now has an existential
  upper-concentration-rate form.  The checked theorem
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_explicitCatalanDiagramBound_varianceTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_branchEnvelopeDominations_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontier_k3`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:7978`
  replaces a named rate constant \(c_{\mathrm{upper}}\) by the statement that

- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`.
  Dependency inspection of the new adapter reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the endpoint asked for a particular exponential-rate witness even though the
  proof only uses existence of one positive rate.
- Challenge After:
  the endpoint states the concentration input at the right granularity:
  prove an exponential bound for the full-model moment bad event at some
  positive rate; the numerical witness is internal bookkeeping.

- The finite-diagram length-three upper endpoint now has a version where the
  mean comparison is generated from scalar Catalan information.  The checked
  theorem
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_explicitCatalanDiagramBound_varianceTail_upperExponentialConcentration_upperMeanScaledDeficit_branchEnvelopeDominations_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontier_k3`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:7880`
  takes the deleted-column finite-diagram estimate, converts it to
  \[
    \left|m_{\mathrm{del}}(d)-(1+3/\lambda)\right| \le D/d,
  \]
  and combines it with
  \[
    d\left((1+3/\lambda)-m_{\mathrm{upper}}(d)\right)\to+\infty.
  \]
  The raw eventual inequality
  \(m_{\mathrm{upper}}(d)\le m_{\mathrm{del}}(d)\) is no longer a separate
  input on this route.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`.
  Dependency inspection of the new converter and endpoint reports only
  `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the finite-diagram variance-facing upper route still asked for a bare mean
  comparison, hiding why that comparison should follow from the Catalan-side
  estimates.
- Challenge After:
  the route exposes the meaningful scalar work instead: prove the explicit
  deleted-column \(D/d\) Catalan estimate and the divergent upper Catalan
  deficit; the mean comparison follows by order bookkeeping.

- The finite-diagram length-three upper endpoint now has a variance-facing
  background version.  The checked theorem
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_explicitCatalanDiagramBound_varianceTail_upperExponentialConcentration_meanCompare_branchEnvelopeDominations_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontier_k3`

- Verification:

  Dependency inspection of the new adapter reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  this finite-diagram upper route still exposed an exponential deleted-moment
  tail even though its background-control need is supplied by the
  Chebyshev-scale \(d^{-4}\) variance estimate.
- Challenge After:
  the route now states the background input at the correct mathematical level:
  prove the \(d^{-4}\) variance estimate, and the older tail predicate is

  upper full-model concentration, mean comparison, and mixed-word frontier.

- The diagnostic length-three upper routes with canonical word bounds and

  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_varianceTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_canonicalWordBounds_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`
  and
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_varianceTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanCatalanMargin_branchEnvelopeDominations_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`
  at

- Challenge Before:
  the diagnostic canonical-word and branch-envelope routes still carried the

- The late length-three upper mixed-word routes now inherit the variance-facing

  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_varianceTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_modelMixedWordBound_mixedTermLimit_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`
  and
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_varianceTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_modelMixedWordBound_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`

- The length-three repaired upper route now has a variance-facing background

- The lower mean-limit layer now has variance-facing PT Wick-core adapters.

  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_noReferenceClosedTraceAndPTWickCoreFrontierInputs_varianceMomentTail`
  and

- Challenge Before:
  the central PT Wick-core lower branch still had a wrapper whose background

- Challenge After:
  the same branch can now be read as: survivor analysis, mixed local expansion,
  and the \(d^{-4}\) deleted-background variance estimate.  The broad
  Gaussian/radial witness and Chebyshev conversion are internal interface
  steps, not separate mathematical frontiers.

- The no-reference closed-trace lower endpoint now has the same
  variance-facing background concentration form as the active endpoint.  The

  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_noReferenceClosedTraceFrontierInputs_meanBound_varianceMomentTail`

  The unit-profile trace-stability side is supplied internally by the closed

- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerNoInputsConcreteChoices`.

- Challenge Before:
  the clean no-reference lower endpoint still exposed the grouped
  Wick/Chebyshev tail predicate, even though the same branch had already closed

- Challenge After:
  the visible no-reference lower inputs are now closer to the paper mathematics:

  \(d^{-4}\)-variance theorem.  The grouped tail predicate is just an internal
  Chebyshev conversion on this branch too.

- The active lower endpoint now has a direct paper-facing variance-tail

  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_activeFrontierInputs_varianceMomentTail`

  in its paper-facing form.  The Chebyshev conversion from this variance bound

  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerNoInputsConcreteChoices`.

  the active lower endpoint still appeared to require the grouped predicate
  `lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMomentSecondMomentWickDeviationTailBound`.
  That was correct internally, but it obscured the ordinary mathematical input:
  a variance estimate strong enough for Chebyshev at tolerance \(1/d\).
- Challenge After:
  the visible active lower concentration input is the paper-facing variance
  statement.  The remaining theorem-strength work is to prove that variance
  estimate for the deleted-column background moment, not to justify an extra
  packaging layer.

- The length-three upper/lower mean comparison now has a direct adapter from
  the lower deleted-column \(D/d\) Catalan estimate plus a divergent scaled
  upper Catalan deficit.  The checked theorems
  `AppendixB.upperLowerConcreteMeanThreeCatalanSignedGap_of_lowerDOverD_and_scaledDeficit`
  and
  `AppendixB.upperLowerConcreteMeanCompare_three_of_lowerDOverD_and_scaledDeficit`

    d(c_3-m_{\mathrm{upper}}(d))\to+\infty

  imply the signed gap packet and hence
  \(m_{\mathrm{upper}}(d)\le m_{\mathrm{del}}(d)\) eventually.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`.
  Axiom audit of both new adapters reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the proof graph had the scalar route in two steps: scaled upper deficit
  gave an upper margin, and the upper margin plus lower \(D/d\) control gave
  the signed gap.  The live input could still look like the intermediate
  margin predicate.
- Challenge After:
  the mean-comparison frontier is clearer.  The lower side is the existing
  deleted-column \(D/d\) Catalan estimate; the remaining upper-side theorem is
  the divergent scaled deficit below the Catalan center.

- The polar/Lévy package now exposes its field content explicitly.  The checked
  projection facts
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.concreteRemainingPolarLevyInputs_polarLaw`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.concreteRemainingPolarLevyInputs_surfaceLevy`,

  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBLevyPolarBridge.lean:1436` show that the
  concrete package fills the polar-law field with the already proved Gaussian

- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBLevyPolarBridge`.
  Axiom audit of the three new projection facts reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  `RemainingPolarLevyInputs` has two fields, so it could be read as two
  geometric/probabilistic assumptions: a polar-law assumption plus a surface
  Lévy assumption.
- Challenge After:
  the concrete constructor makes the split explicit.  The polar law is closed
  by the Gaussian direction/surface-measure identity; the only live input in
  this package is the global surface Lévy concentration theorem itself.

- The repaired lower mixed envelope now has direct adapters from the older
  explicit-error envelope.  The checked theorems
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedLocalExpansionEnvelopeOnSphereWithError_of_withError`
  and
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedLocalExpansionEnvelopeOnSphereWithError_lowerConcreteMixedError`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:1615`
  show that a mixed estimate proved on the favourable event without using the
  Frobenius-sphere support immediately supplies the repaired
  sphere-supported endpoint, and that the old fixed \(1/d\) budget is the
  corresponding special case.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`.
  Axiom audit of both new adapters reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the repaired lower endpoint consumes the sphere-supported mixed envelope,
  while some older local-expansion statements are phrased without the extra
  sphere hypothesis.  This made the repair look like it introduced a separate
  mixed theorem whenever an older, stronger envelope was already available.
- Challenge After:
  that interface mismatch is closed.  The real mixed-word work remains the
  local expansion/scalar comparison itself; the extra Frobenius-sphere support
  in the repaired endpoint is no additional obstacle when a stronger envelope
  has been proved.

- The lower reference-cone input now has an explicitly named closed concrete
  theorem.  The checked theorem
  `AppendixB.lower_referenceCone_BipIndex_Fin_eventually_concreteChoices_closed`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerReferenceConeConcreteChoices.lean:458`
  supplies, eventually in \(d\), the reference projective cap formula for every
  coordinate \(i_0\in[d]\times[d]\):
  \[
    \mu\{|\langle e_{i_0},x\rangle|^2\ge 1-r^2\}
      =
    r^{2(N-1)}.
  \]
  The proof splits off the distinguished coordinate as
  \(\mathbf{1}\oplus\kappa\), uses the spherical block-mass
  \(\mathrm{Beta}(1,N-1)\) law, and transports the result along a finite
  coordinate equivalence.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerReferenceConeConcreteChoices`.
  Axiom audit of the closed concrete theorem reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  downstream endpoints already consumed a local reference theorem, but the
  name still looked like an `hReference`-style input.  It was too easy to leave
  the reference-cone formula in the mental list of remaining assumptions.
- Challenge After:
  the lower reference-cone geometry is explicitly closed.  The remaining lower
  endpoint debts are mean convergence, mixed-word estimates, and
  deleted-background moment tails, not the projective cap reference formula.

- The lower unit-profile split trace-power components are now named as closed
  finite-dimensional facts.  The checked theorems
  `AppendixB.lowerLeftReducedDensityDiagonalPowerLeTracePower_closed` and
  `AppendixB.lowerRankOneProjectorGammaTracePowerDominatesLeftReducedDensityTracePower_closed`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerUnitProfileConcreteChoices.lean:914`
  and
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerUnitProfileConcreteChoices.lean:1077`
  close the two local pieces
  \[
    (\rho_{ii})^k \le \operatorname{Tr}(\rho^k),
    \qquad
    \operatorname{Tr}(\rho^k)
      \le
    \operatorname{Tr}(((uu^*)^\Gamma)^k)
  \]
  in the rank-one spike profile route.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerUnitProfileConcreteChoices`.
  Axiom audit of both new closed split components reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the core spectral proofs were already present, and the combined unit-profile
  theorem was closed, but the two split frontier names still lacked direct
  closed theorem labels.  A reader of the split endpoint could mistake those
  local trace-power components for remaining assumptions.
- Challenge After:
  the split unit-profile layer is explicitly closed.  The lower endpoint still
  has real theorem-strength inputs on the mean, mixed, and background-tail
  sides, but not on this rank-one spike profile trace-power layer.

- The PT Catalan mean notation is now explicitly pinned to the existing lower
  Catalan-series target and to the closed-form moment polynomial.  The checked
  aliases
  `AppendixB.ptCatalanMean_eq_lowerDeletedBackgroundMeanCatalanLimit`,
  `AppendixB.ptCatalanMean_three`, and
  `AppendixB.ptCatalanMean_eq_invPow_closedFormMoment` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:61`
  say, respectively, that
  \[
    \mathrm{ptCatalanMean}(k,\lambda)
      =
    \mathrm{lowerDeletedBackgroundMeanCatalanLimit}(\lambda,k),
  \]
  that the \(k=3\) center is \(1+3\lambda^{-1}\), and that for
  \(\lambda\ne0\)
  \[
    \mathrm{ptCatalanMean}(k,\lambda)
      =
    \lambda^{-k}\,\mathrm{ClosedFormDet.M}(\lambda,k).
  \]
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`.
  Axiom audit of the three new aliases reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  `ptCatalanMean` was definitionally an alias, but the useful mathematical
  equalities were not named.  A reader of the endpoint signatures could still
  mistake the PT Catalan notation, the lower Catalan-series target, and the
  closed-form determinant moment as separate inputs.
- Challenge After:
  those targets are now visibly the same scalar target.  The remaining theorem
  strength is the deleted-column spherical moment convergence to that target,
  not any extra Catalan-polynomial identification.

- The lower deleted-background Wick/Chebyshev paper-facing names now have
  explicit conversion constructors in both directions against the grouped
  endpoint frontier.  The new checked adapters
  `AppendixB.deletedColumnSphericalMoment_secondMoment_expansion_of_secondMomentWickDeviationTailBound`,
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMomentSecondMomentWickDeviationTailBound_of_secondMoment_expansion`,
  `AppendixB.deletedColumnSphericalMoment_variance_le_const_div_d4_of_secondMomentWickDeviationTailBound`,

  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerBackgroundMomentConcreteChoices.lean:504`
  make explicit that the paper-facing two-trace expansion name, variance name,
  and \(1/d\) deviation-tail name are currently aliases for the single grouped
  Wick/Chebyshev frontier
  `lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMomentSecondMomentWickDeviationTailBound R k`.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerBackgroundMomentConcreteChoices`.
  Axiom audit of all four new adapters reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the file already had iff statements, but not every useful conversion
  direction was named.  Endpoint readers could still treat the two-trace
  expansion, variance estimate, and \(1/d\) tail as a stack of separate Lean
  inputs at this abstraction layer.
- Challenge After:
  the named constructors show exactly what is packaging and what is theorem
  strength: the grouped Wick/Chebyshev frontier is the remaining input here;
  the paper-facing labels are aliases until the internal split is formalized
  below that frontier.

- The upper moment concentration scale-bound alias is now explicitly connected
  to the general bad-set input.  The checked theorems
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteMomentBadSetScaleBound_iff_badSetBound_scale`,
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteMomentBadSetScaleBound.of_badSetBound_scale`, and
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteMomentBadSetScaleBound.to_badSetBound_scale` at
  `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:7696` say that
  \[
    \mathrm{UpperConcreteMomentBadSetScaleBound}(C,k)
  \]
  is exactly
  \[
    \mathrm{UpperConcreteMomentBadSetBound}
      (\mathrm{upperConcreteMomentBoundScale}(C),k).
  \]
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBUpperBoundClosure`.
  Axiom audit of all three new adapters reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the upper background-moment frontier had both a general bad-set bound name and
  a canonical-scale bound name.  The relation was definitional in code, but not
  recorded as theorem-level evidence, so it could be misread as two separate
  concentration inputs.
- Challenge After:
  the scale-bound name is proved to be only the general moment bad-set input
  with the canonical envelope plugged in.  The remaining probabilistic work is
  the moment concentration estimate itself, not an additional scale-interface
  obligation.

- The real-sphere geometric handoff target is now explicitly identified with
  the downstream aliases.  The checked theorems
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.sphere_caps_minimize_neighborhoods_noInput_target_iff_FullSphericalIsoperimetry`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.FullSphericalIsoperimetry_of_sphere_caps_minimize_neighborhoods_noInput_target`,
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.sphere_caps_minimize_neighborhoods_noInput_target_of_FullSphericalIsoperimetry`,
  and
  `PptFactorization.AppendixB.sphere_caps_minimize_neighborhoods_noInput_target_iff_fullSphericalIsoperimetry`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFullSphericalIsoperimetry.lean:80`
  make the alias situation explicit:
  \[
    \mathrm{sphere\_caps\_minimize\_neighborhoods\_noInput\_target}
    \Longleftrightarrow
    \mathrm{FullSphericalIsoperimetry}.
  \]
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFullSphericalIsoperimetry`.
  Axiom audit of all four new adapters reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the handoff file defined the exact no-input target, while downstream code
  consumed `FullSphericalIsoperimetry` or its lower-camel-case compatibility
  alias.  This was logically harmless but made the frontier look like it might
  contain several geometric inputs.
- Challenge After:
  the target file itself now proves these are the same proposition.  The
  remaining geometry is one theorem, not an alias stack: the finite real-sphere
  cap-minimization/isoperimetry statement.

- The concrete upper `hIso` obligation now has direct adapters from the exact
  no-input real-sphere target.  The checked theorems
  `AppendixB.upper_hIso_concreteModel_pointwise_of_sphere_caps_minimize_neighborhoods`
  and
  `AppendixB.eventually_upper_hIso_concreteModel_of_sphere_caps_minimize_neighborhoods`

  closes the pointwise and eventual sharp spherical-isoperimetry hypotheses for

- Challenge Before:
  the board said the real-sphere theorem was the no-input geometric target,

  obligation with a separate geometric input.
- Challenge After:
  there is no extra model-law, polar-law, or dimension input hidden behind
  `hIso`.  The transport to the concrete model is closed; the remaining
  geometric theorem is exactly the classical finite-sphere cap-minimization
  statement itself.

- On the current upper endpoint, the canonical-envelope word-bound side is now

  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_secondMomentWickTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_canonicalWordBounds_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`

  the current sharp mixed-case endpoint still asked for an arbitrary

- Challenge After:

- The current upper endpoint now splits the bundled upper mixed scalar-limit
  input into its three defect cases.  The endpoint companion
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_secondMomentWickTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_modelMixedWordBound_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`

    T_{1L}(A,L_1)+T_{1Q}(A,Q_1)+T_{\ge 2}(A,L_2,Q_2).
  \]
  In plain math, every mixed word is classified as a one-linear-defect word, a

- Challenge After:

- The current upper endpoint now exposes the upper mixed-remainder input as a

  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_secondMomentWickTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_modelMixedWordBound_mixedTermLimit_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`

- Challenge Before:
  the sharp endpoint still contained the broad local-spike predicate
  \(\mathrm{UpperMixedRemainder}\), hiding the actual word-level work.
- Challenge After:

- The upper mean-margin input now has a compact sufficient asymptotic form.  The
  definition
  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModelMeanThreeCatalanScaledDeficitTendsToTop` and the
  theorem
  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModelMeanThreeCatalanUpperMarginFor_of_scaledDeficitTendsToTop`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:382`
  and
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:403`
  record the scalar implication
  \[
    d\bigl((1+3\lambda^{-1})-m_{\rm upper}(d)\bigr)\to+\infty
    \Longrightarrow
    \forall D\ge 0,\ \exists E\ge D,\quad
    \forall^\mathrm{eventual} d,\quad
    m_{\rm upper}(d)\le (1+3\lambda^{-1})-E/d .
  \]
  In plain math, the arbitrary upper margin is now reduced to proving that the
  signed deficit from the Catalan center dominates every multiple of \(1/d\).
  The endpoint companion
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_secondMomentWickTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanScaledDeficit_upperMixedRemainder_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`
  consumes this asymptotic directly, so the older for-every-\(D\) margin is no
  longer the cleanest public frontier for the mean side.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`
  and audits of the new scalar adapter and endpoint companion.  The audits
  report only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the upper mean-margin input was written as a for-every-\(D\) eventual margin,
  which made the exact upper-model asymptotic needed on the mean side hard to
  see.
- Challenge After:
  the scalar bookkeeping is checked and wired into an endpoint companion.  The
  remaining theorem-strength leaf is the model-specific asymptotic
  \[
    d\bigl((1+3\lambda^{-1})-m_{\rm upper}(d)\bigr)\to+\infty .
  \]

- The upper mean-margin input has a checked scalar diagnostic at the actual
  length-three Catalan center.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.exact_upper_catalan_center_does_not_force_upperMarginForAll` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:331`
  proves that even exact centering
  \[
    \forall^\mathrm{eventual} d,\quad
    |u_d-(1+3\lambda^{-1})|\le 0/d
  \]
  does not imply the signed below-center margin
  \[
    \forall D\ge 0,\ \exists E\ge D,\quad
    \forall^\mathrm{eventual} d,\quad
    u_d\le (1+3\lambda^{-1})-E/d .
  \]
  In plain math, an absolute Catalan-centering estimate, even with zero error,
  is the wrong direction of information for the repaired upper mean comparison.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`
  and audit of the new theorem.  The audit reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the upper mean-margin input could still be misread as a routine Catalan
  centering estimate around \(1+3\lambda^{-1}\).
- Challenge After:
  the actual Catalan center now has a checked counterexample to that inference.
  The input needs signed below-center \(1/d\) information, not merely two-sided
  or exact Catalan centering.

- The branchwise upper-mixed companion route has checked no-go theorems for all
  three branch pairs.  The theorems
  `AppendixB.upper_concrete_branchEnvelope_meanMargin_endpoint_oneLinear_input_impossible_k3`,
  `AppendixB.upper_concrete_branchEnvelope_meanMargin_endpoint_oneQuadratic_input_impossible_k3`,
  and
  `AppendixB.upper_concrete_branchEnvelope_meanMargin_endpoint_multiDefect_input_impossible_k3`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:7039`,
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:7059`,
  and
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:7079`
  prove that each domination-plus-zero-limit branch pair exposed by the
  branchwise endpoint is inconsistent:
  \[
    D_{1L}+T_{1L}\Longrightarrow \bot,\qquad
    D_{1Q}+T_{1Q}\Longrightarrow \bot,\qquad
    D_{\ge 2}+T_{\ge 2}\Longrightarrow \bot .
  \]
  In plain math, if an abstract branch envelope dominates the corresponding
  actual-model canonical branch term and that same abstract envelope tends to
  zero, then it would force a divergent canonical term to be eventually below
  something tending to zero.
- Verification for these branch no-go theorems:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`
  and audits of the three new theorems.  The audits report only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  after splitting the broad upper mixed input, the branchwise endpoint could
  still look like a live proof route with six explicit inputs.
- Challenge After:
  the route is diagnosed as impossible in each branch pair.  The upper
  local-spike proof cannot be closed by this domination-plus-zero-limit
  interface; it needs a different envelope mechanism or a different local spike
  argument.

- The current upper-mean-margin endpoint now has a branchwise upper-mixed

  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_secondMomentWickTail_upperExponentialConcentrationExists_upperMeanCatalanMargin_branchEnvelopeDominations_caseTermLimits_lowerMixedErrorFrontierExists_k3`

- The average-form auto-height-band geometry input now has a no-extra-tail
  full-isoperimetry package through the cap-cone flattening map.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.fullSphericalIsoperimetry_of_finRealSphereAutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:208`
  proves

  PptFactorization.AppendixB.FullSphericalIsoperimetry`.  In plain math, this
  packages the two geometric ingredients as
  \[
    \text{auto-height bands}
      +
    \text{cap-cone flattening tail}
      \Longrightarrow
    \text{full spherical isoperimetry}.
  \]
  The tail side is supplied by the already checked chain
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_from_capConeFlatteningMap`
  and
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_cosinePowerTailBelowHalf`.

- Challenge After:

- The auto-height-band geometry input now supplies the global half-measure
  hemisphere comparison.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparison_of_finRealSphereAutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup`
  at

  proves
  `finRealSphereAutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup ->
  sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparison`.  In plain math, the existing
  polarization argument gives the cap comparison for all dimensions `n >= 2`,
  and the already checked elementary adapter supplies the `n = 1` case:
  \[
    \text{auto-height bands}
      \Longrightarrow
    \text{hemispheres maximize }\mu((A_r)^c)
      \text{ among half-mass } A .
  \]
- Verification for this global hemisphere-comparison bridge:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`
  and audit of the new theorem.  The audit reports only `propext`,

- Challenge Before:
  the board only recorded the dimension-`n >= 2` cap comparison.  The global
  half-measure statement still needed the one-dimensional adapter to match the
  endpoint-facing geometric input.
- Challenge After:
  that adapter is now connected.  The remaining geometric theorem-strength
  work is not this bridge; it is the separate Gaussian hemisphere-tail input
  needed to upgrade hemisphere comparison to full spherical isoperimetry.

- The existential upper full-model concentration input now has its direct
  small-probability consequence recorded.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound.exists_tendsto_zero`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:5590`
  proves that
  `exists c, UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound R c k`
  implies, for every fixed positive `slack`, that the centered full-model
  background deviation probability tends to zero.  In plain math, once
  `P(B_d^{mom}) <= exp(-c d^2)` holds for some `c > 0`, the bad moment mass is
  `o(1)`.
- Verification for this existential concentration consequence:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`
  and audit of the new theorem.  The audit reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the active route asked for an existential exponential rate, while the board
  only recorded the fixed-rate squeeze.
- Challenge After:
  the proof graph now records exactly what the existential input buys:
  eventual smallness of the upper full-model moment-deviation event.  Proving
  the exponential concentration estimate itself remains theorem-strength work.

- The same existential upper concentration input now also has the strict
  bad-event consequence needed by the background typical set.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound.exists_badSet_tendsto_zero`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:5697` proves that
  \[
    \exists c>0,\quad
    \mathbb P(B_d^{\mathrm{mom,closed}})\le e^{-c d^2}
    \quad\Longrightarrow\quad
    \mathbb P(B_d^{\mathrm{mom}})\to 0 .
  \]
  In plain math, the closed absolute-deviation bound controls the strict moment
  bad set by inclusion, then the exponential envelope tends to zero.
- Verification for this strict bad-set consequence:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBUpperBoundClosure` and audit of the new
  theorem.  The audit reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and
  `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the upper concentration input had a recorded closed-deviation consequence, but
  the strict moment bad event used in the typical set was still implicit.
- Challenge After:
  that strict-event plumbing is closed.  The remaining theorem-strength work is
  proving the exponential concentration estimate itself, not passing from the
  closed deviation event to the strict bad event.

- The length-three lower Catalan `D / d` input now has its plain convergence
  consequence recorded.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMeanHasCatalanLimit_three_of_threeCatalanDOverDBound_atBalancedRatio`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:1141`
  proves that
  `lowerDeletedColumnBackgroundMomentThreeCatalanDOverDBound_atBalancedRatio R`
  implies
  `lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMeanHasCatalanLimit R 3`.  In plain math,
  the quantitative estimate
  `|m_deleted(d) - (1 + 3 * R.lam^-1)| <= D / d`
  is stronger than mere convergence of the deleted-column mean to the
  length-three Catalan center.
- Verification for this Catalan `D / d` consequence:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`
  and audit of the new theorem.  The audit reports only `propext`,
  `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the balanced length-three Catalan input could still read like just another
  mean-limit assumption.
- Challenge After:
  the proof graph records its exact strength: it implies the old mean-limit
  input by scalar squeeze, while retaining the real theorem-strength content
  as the crossing/spherical `D / d` estimate itself.

- The lower deleted-background Wick/Chebyshev input now has explicit
  small-probability consequences.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMomentSecondMomentWickDeviationTailBound.eventuallySmall`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerBackgroundMomentConcreteChoices.lean:384`
  proves that the closed deleted-background deviation event has probability
  tending to zero at the concrete tolerance: for every fixed `a`, `slack`, and
  `eta > 0`, eventually its spherical probability is at most `eta`.  The
  theorem
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMomentSecondMomentWickDeviationTailBound.badSetEventuallySmall`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerBackgroundMomentConcreteChoices.lean:643`
  gives the same `o(1)` conclusion for the strict moment bad set by combining
  the closed-event bound with the strict/closed inclusion.
- Verification for these Wick/Chebyshev consequences:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices`
  and audits of both new theorems.  The audits report only `propext`,

- Challenge Before:
  `lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMomentSecondMomentWickDeviationTailBound R 3`
  still looked like an unreadable probability assumption in the upper route.
- Challenge After:
  the proof graph now records the exact use of that input:
  `P(|M_d - E M_d| >= tau_d) <= C / d^2` implies both the closed deviation
  set and the strict moment bad set have probability `o(1)`.  The
  theorem-strength work remains proving the Wick/variance estimate that
  supplies the constant `C`; the scalar squeeze and strict-event plumbing are
  closed.

- The lower mixed-error frontier has been unpacked and connected to the
  endpoint-facing existential shape.  The projections
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier.envelope` and
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier.eventuallySmall` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3557`
  and
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3569`
  show that the frontier is exactly a pair: a sphere-supported local

  The wrapper
  `AppendixB.exists_lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier_of_PTScaleComparisons` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3717`

- Challenge Before:
  `∃ errMix, lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier R k eps errMix` could still read

- Challenge After:
  the packaging is closed: from the two PT scale comparisons one gets exactly

- The upper full-model exponential concentration input has a checked
  zero-limit consequence.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound.tendsto_zero`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:5553` proves that
  `UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound R c k` implies, for
  every fixed `slack > 0`,
  `mu(backgroundMomentDeviationSet upperCanonicalTau) -> 0`.  The proof is
  pure scalar squeezing from the assumed bound
  `mu(...) <= exp (-(c * d^2))` and the checked fact that this envelope tends
  to zero when `c > 0`.
- Verification for this concentration consequence:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBUpperBoundClosure` and audit of
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound.tendsto_zero`.
  The audit reports only `propext`, `Classical.choice`, and `Quot.sound`.
- Challenge Before:
  the upper concentration input still looked like a named wrapper around a
  probability estimate.
- Challenge After:
  the board records the exact mathematical force of the input: it is a genuine
  exponential concentration theorem for the full spherical model.  Proving the
  estimate itself remains theorem-strength work; mathlib only supplies the
  scalar squeeze once the probability bound is assumed.

- The average-form auto-height-band geometric input has been connected to the
  cap-comparison core.  The theorem
  `AppendixB.sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_finRealSphereAutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:180`
  proves that `finRealSphereAutoHeightBandsAverageGainSup` implies

  mathlib; it already contains the spherical-polarization/symmetrization
  content needed to identify caps as extremizers.

  `m_upper(d) <= m_deleted(d)`, then

  `lowerConcreteEps eps d = eps`.

  false scalar squeeze packages.  Remaining theorem-strength inputs: geometry,
  ratio-parametric Catalan `D / d`, lower mixed error frontier,
  variance/Chebyshev, mean comparison, upper concentration, and the upper
  mixed-remainder bound.

  one tempting proof strategy was to reuse the canonical fixed-slack
  local-expansion budget under a half-mass typical set.
- Challenge After:
  that proof strategy is now ruled out.  In plain math, `upperCanonicalTau`
  tends to zero like `slack / d`, while the proposed budget keeps a positive
  fixed error allowance below it and simultaneously asks the resulting typical
  set to have mass at least one half.  Those requirements are incompatible.
  The concentration input must therefore come from a different concentration
  mechanism or a budget whose error allowance also shrinks.

- Upper branch domination-plus-limit interface refuted at `k = 3`.  The checked
  proof objects
  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModel_oneLinearDom_and_termLimit_impossible`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:7968`,
  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModel_oneQuadraticDom_and_termLimit_impossible`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:8079`, and
  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModel_multiDefectDom_and_termLimit_impossible`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:8226` show that the
  current upper mixed branch interface is mutually inconsistent for the concrete
  length-three model.  In plain math: each branch asked for an abstract scalar
  package that both dominates the actual-model canonical branch scalar and tends
  to zero.  But the canonical scalar already diverges for the spike slack choice.
  Thus domination plus a zero-limit would force a divergent term to be
  eventually below a term tending to zero.  For the multi-defect branch the
  explicit witness is the pure `L,L,L` word.

- Actual-model multi-defect canonical upper scalar route refuted.  The checked
  proof object
  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModel_multiDefectMixedTermLimit_not_canonical`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean:8044` proves that, for
  `eps > 0`, the actual-model canonical choices
  `upperConcreteModelMixedWordAbound R`,
  `upperConcreteModelMixedWordL2bound R eps 3`, and
  `upperConcreteModelMixedWordQ2bound R eps 3` cannot satisfy
  `UpperConcreteMultiDefectMixedTermLimit 3 ...`.  The witness is the pure
  length-three word `L,L,L`: it is mixed, it is neither a one-linear-defect nor
  a one-quadratic-defect word, and its letter counts are `(A,L,Q) = (0,3,0)`.
  Its canonical scalar package is therefore the multi-defect term
  `N^2 * L1^3` after the actual `L2 = L1` specialization.  The new helper
  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModel_N_spikeRadius_tendsto_atTop` records the
  decisive scale: `(N * radius)^2 = a * d^(8/3)` for positive slack radius
  `a`.  Hence the pure-`L` multi-defect term diverges rather than tending to
  zero.

- Length-three upper actual-model endpoint now states the Catalan mean-side
  input at the actual balanced aspect ratio and consumes all three deterministic
  upper mixed-word branches through local matrix estimates plus scalar
  envelope-domination inputs:
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_varianceStack_upperExponentialConcentration_meanCompare_branchEnvelopeDominations_caseTermLimits_lowerPTDirectScalarCases_normalizedM_k3`
  at `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:5345`.

  `UpperConcreteModelMixedWordBound R eps 3 ...` and the three casewise branch
  word predicates are no longer theorem-facing on this route.  They are
  assembled internally from
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelOneLinearMixedWordBound_of_envelopeDomination`,
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelOneQuadraticMixedWordBound_of_envelopeDomination`,
  and
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelMultiDefectMixedWordBound_of_envelopeDomination`.
  The three scalar envelope-dominations and the three scalar branch-limit
  packages remain visible conditions; this is a genuine frontier sharpening,
  not a proof of those scalar asymptotics.
- The same endpoint still combines the PT direct-scalar lower mixed route with
  direct block-to-`sSup` Lemma 4.3 geometry, normalized PT scalar constant
  `max M 0`, the deleted-column variance/Chebyshev predicate, direct
  upper/deleted mean comparison, and the named upper concentration proposition
  `UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound R cUpper 3`.  The
  older parameterized route remains available as a compatibility wrapper:
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_explicitCatalanRatio_varianceStack_upperExponentialTail_meanCompare_upperMixedWordExplicit_upperScalarLimitsExplicit_lowerPTDirectScalarCases_normalizedM`
  at `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:4645`.
- Challenge Before:
  the length-three manuscript route had already moved the Catalan input to the
  actual balanced aspect ratio, packaged the upper full-model concentration,
  and rerouted the one-linear and one-quadratic word branches.  It still exposed
  the multi-defect deterministic branch word estimate
  `UpperConcreteModelMultiDefectMixedWordBound R eps 3 ...` as a theorem input,
  even though the local multi-defect matrix estimate was already available.
- Challenge After:
  the parameterized sharp PT direct-scalar route remains
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_explicitCatalanRatio_varianceStack_upperExponentialTail_meanCompare_upperMixedWordExplicit_upperScalarLimitsExplicit_lowerPTDirectScalarCases_normalizedM`
  at `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:4645`.
  The manuscript-length route is now
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_capConeFlatteningMap_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_threeCatalanAtBalancedRatio_varianceStack_upperExponentialConcentration_meanCompare_branchEnvelopeDominations_caseTermLimits_lowerPTDirectScalarCases_normalizedM_k3`
  at `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:5345`.
  The previous length-three direct `D / d` wrapper over all limiting ratios
  remains available as a compatibility adapter at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:5116`,
  but it is no longer the article-facing route.
  The previous length-three explicit-diagram wrapper remains available as a
  compatibility adapter at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/UpperFromLowerColumnConcreteChoices.lean:4891`,
  but it is no longer the article-facing route.
  The geometric input is direct: for every half-mass competitor with worse
  neighbourhood-complement mass than the hemisphere by margin `η`, it asks for
  a measurable competitor `Cmodel`, visible symmetric-difference mass, and a
  rectangular block whose certified gain is already the `sSup` of the
  polarization gain set.  The average-to-sup bookkeeping is no longer
  theorem-facing on this route.
  This is a geometry-frontier sharpening, not a proof of Lemma 4.3 itself.
  The lower mixed side now asks for the two direct PT scalar trace estimates at
  the normalized nonnegative constant `max M 0`; the separate `0 ≤ M`
  hypothesis is no longer theorem-facing.
  The upper full-model concentration input is now the named proposition
  `UpperConcreteModelMomentExponentialDeviationSetBound R cUpper 3`.  Its
  definition is exactly `0 < cUpper` plus, for every fixed positive slack, an
  eventual bound of the closed background-moment deviation probability by
  `exp (-(cUpper*d^2))`.  This wrapper does not prove that concentration
  estimate.
  The lower deleted-background side no longer asks for an exponential
  deleted-column tail on this sharp route.  It asks directly for the
  variance/Chebyshev Wick predicate
  `deletedColumnSphericalMoment_variance_le_const_div_d4 R k`.  Older
  exponential-tail compatibility wrappers remain above this route.
  The lower one-column half-mass step consumes the variance/Chebyshev predicate
  directly through
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMomentSecondMomentWickDeviationTailBound_of_deletedColumnSphericalMoment_variance_le_const_div_d4`.
  Literal PT termwise smallness is still internal via
  `AppendixB.lowerPartialTransposeMixedWordBoundD_termwise_tendsto`; the exact
  finite-budget predicate is still supplied internally by
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedWordBudgetWithExactSum`, and exact finite-sum
  smallness is supplied internally by
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedErrorEventuallySmall_of_filteredSum_termwise_tendsto`.
  The length-three finite-diagram mean-side input is now model-specific:
  there is some `D ≥ 0` such that eventually
  `|lowerDeletedColumnBackgroundMomentSequence R.sample 3 d -
      (1 + 3 * R.lam⁻¹)| ≤ D / d`.
  The ratio-parametric wrapper is recovered internally using uniqueness of
  limits and the checked scalar fact
  `(R.sample d - 1) / lowerConcreteN d → R.lam`.
  This wrapper proves the scalar center and adapter bookkeeping, but it does
  not prove that crossing-pairing/spherical-correction estimate.
  The mean comparison input no longer hides the upper side behind
  `upperConcreteModelMeanSeq`: it asks directly for the concrete full-model
  spherical integral
  `upperConcreteMean (LeftIndex d) (RightIndex d) (SampleIndex (R.sample d))
    k d`
  to be eventually bounded by `lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMean R k d`.
  This wrapper does not prove that full-vs-deleted mean comparison.
  The actual-model upper mixed-word input is now split into three scalar
  domination propositions:
  `UpperConcreteModelOneLinearMixedWordEnvelopeDomination R eps 3 ...`,
  `UpperConcreteModelOneQuadraticMixedWordEnvelopeDomination R eps 3 ...`, and
  `UpperConcreteModelMultiDefectMixedWordEnvelopeDomination R eps 3 ...`, all
  defined in `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean`.  These
  dominations say that the concrete bounds produced by the checked local matrix
  estimates are eventually below the caller's chosen envelopes.  The
  `backgroundTypicalSet` membership still expands to the three clauses:
  centered moment within `upperCanonicalTau slack d`, sample operator norm at
  most `upperConcreteM slack d / sqrt (upperConcreteN d)`, and
  partial-transpose/Gamma operator norm at most
  `upperConcreteM slack d / upperConcreteN d`.  This is a theorem-strength
  refinement for all three deterministic upper mixed-word branches: the branch
  word theorems are no longer theorem-facing, but the scalar dominations needed
  to use the local matrix estimates are still visible conditions.
  The proof path from the three branch envelope-dominations and the three
  scalar limits to `UpperConcreteModelMixedRemainderBound R eps 3` now uses the
  named adapter
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelMixedRemainderBound_of_branchEnvelopeDominations_and_caseTermLimits`
  in `PptFactorization/AppendixBUpperBoundClosure.lean`; the sharp endpoint
  calls that deterministic mixed-remainder closure directly.
  The one-linear, one-quadratic, and multi-defect branch suppliers
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelOneLinearMixedWordBound_of_envelopeDomination`,
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelOneQuadraticMixedWordBound_of_envelopeDomination`,
  `AppendixB.UpperConcreteModelMultiDefectMixedWordBound_of_envelopeDomination`,
  and the sharp endpoint all audit to `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.
  The scalar upper mixed-envelope limits are now the named packages
  `UpperConcreteOneLinearMixedTermLimit`, `UpperConcreteOneQuadraticMixedTermLimit`,
  and `UpperConcreteMultiDefectMixedTermLimit` at `k = 3`.  Their definitions
  are the same three `Tendsto` statements: one-linear, one-quadratic, and the
  remaining multi-defect words after excluding the first two cases.  This
  wrapper does not prove those asymptotic scalar limits.
  On the length-three endpoint the remaining live upper inputs are the direct
  Lemma 4.3 packet, the direct third-moment `D / d` Catalan estimate around
  `1 + 3 * R.lam⁻¹`,
  the two lower mixed direct scalar PT trace estimates at `max M 0` and
  `k = 3`, the deleted-column variance/Chebyshev Wick predicate at `k = 3`,
  raw upper/deleted mean comparison at `k = 3`, the upper full-model
  exponential concentration proposition at `k = 3`, the one-linear,
  one-quadratic, and multi-defect scalar envelope-domination conditions at
  `k = 3`, and the three scalar upper mixed branch-limit packages at `k = 3`.
  The parameterized wrapper carries the same leaves at arbitrary `k ≥ 3`.
  Survivor analysis remains an alternative supplier route for the bounded-mean input,
  not a direct hypothesis of this sharp upper endpoint.
- Diagnostic:
  the clean explicit-Catalan/variance-tail upper wrapper now has a direct-PT
  scalar companion:
  `AppendixB.upper_eventual_from_concrete_model_of_averageHeightBands_lowerCanonicalColumnPipeline_explicitCatalanDiagramBound_varianceTail_upperExponentialTail_upperMeanScaledDeficit_upperMixedRemainder_lowerPTDirectScalarCases_k3`.
  It has the same upper conclusion as the adjacent
  `...lowerPTScaleComparisons_k3` compatibility endpoint, but obtains the
  existential lower mixed frontier through
  `AppendixB.exists_lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier_of_PTDirectScalarCases`.

  `AppendixB.upperConcreteModelM_div_upperConcreteN_eventually_ge_real` says it

- Verification:

2026-06-02:

- Upper Appendix B graduate-counting bridge is now specialized to Aubrun's
  explicit polynomial:
  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_graduate_relation_counting_canonical_explicitQ`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBPipelineGraduate.lean:600`.
- Challenge Before:
  the shortest canonical envelope bridge still had a generic polynomial `Q`
  and the scalar side conditions `hdQ` and `hsQ` needed for real powers.  For
  Aubrun's paper polynomial these side conditions are pure nonnegativity
  bookkeeping.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now specializes `Q` to `aubrunProposition71Q C0`, i.e.
  `Q(k)=C0*k*(2*k)^36`, and discharges the scalar side conditions from
  `0 ≤ C0` using `AppendixB.aubrunProposition71Q_nonneg`.  The remaining
  theorem-strength upper input is exactly the graduate relation-counting
  estimate for this explicit polynomial:
  `AubrunGraduateRelationCounting (aubrunProposition71Q C0) (dNat : ℝ)
  (sNat : ℝ) (aubrunEvenMomentParameter dNat - 1)`.  Scalar conditions
  `0<dNat`, `0<sNat`, `0<lam`, and `lam*dNat^2≤sNat` remain as model/regime
  assumptions.  This still does not prove Aubrun's combinatorial
  relation-counting theorem or full PPT.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBPipelineGraduate`,
  `lake env lean /tmp/check_pipeline_canonical_explicitQ.lean`, and
  `lake build PptFactorization` pass.  The new endpoint audits to `[propext,
  Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; touched Lean files contain no whole-word
  `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`; `git diff --check` is clean.

- Lower length-three endpoint now replaces the broad PT variance/Chebyshev
  input by the stronger exponential-deviation frontier:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_paperFacingPTCatalanErrorMeanAndExponentialTailStack_k3_withMixedErrorComponents_splitMixedWordBudget`

  The remaining lower theorem-strength inputs are the length-three

  `AppendixB.gammaExpectation_pipeline_to_spherical_bound_of_graduate_relation_counting`
  at `PptFactorization/AppendixBPipelineGraduate.lean:36`.
- Challenge Before:
  the checked expectation/spherical pipeline consumed an abstract
  `AubrunOffDiagonalExpectationDerivation` package.  The graduate
  relation-counting file could construct that package from concrete
  `Fin dNat`, `Fin sNat` counting/envelope/integrability inputs, but callers
  still had to pass through the intermediate package explicitly.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now connects graduate relation counting directly to the existing
  Appendix B expectation pipeline.  Supplying the graduate counting hypothesis,
  the off-diagonal integrability and scalar envelope/model comparison inputs,
  the diagonal expectation bound, and the radial/spherical normalization inputs
  yields the off-diagonal mean bound, full gamma mean bound, quadratic lift
  mean bound, and spherical gamma mean bound in one checked theorem.  This is

- Upper fixed-moment bulk almost-positivity now has a finite-rate deviation
  adapter:
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_bad_event_measure_le_of_centered_moment_deviation_event_measure_le_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:516`.
- Challenge Before:
  the deviation-form upper adapter was asymptotic: convergence in probability
  of the fixed centered moment implied convergence of the combined bad event.
  A concrete concentration theorem with an explicit dimensionwise rate still
  needed an extra monotonicity wrapper.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now transfers a dimensionwise bound on the natural deviation event
  `η / 2 < |moment_average - catalan/λ^m|` directly to the combined bad event
  where either the negative eigenvalue fraction or the normalized negative
  trace mass exceeds `η`.  The existing tendsto adapter now reuses this
  finite-rate theorem.  The live theorem-strength input remains the actual
  fixed-order concentration estimate.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  Axiom audits for
  both the finite-rate adapter and the tendsto companion report only
  `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

- Upper fixed-moment bulk almost-positivity now accepts the natural deviation
  concentration input:
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_tendsto_bad_event_measure_zero_of_centered_moment_deviation_event_tendsto_zero_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:572`.
- Challenge Before:
  the dependent-index fixed-moment bulk adapter transferred a threshold-event
  probability bound to the combined bad event, but fixed-order concentration is
  usually stated as convergence in probability of the normalized centered
  moment to its Catalan limit.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now converts that natural deviation event,
  `η / 2 < |moment_average - catalan/λ^m|`, into the same combined
  almost-positivity bad event for spectra indexed by `ι d`.  Thus a future
  fixed-order concentration theorem stated in deviation form directly implies
  Level 1 bulk almost-positivity.  This still does not prove the concentration
  theorem, full PPT, or any Aubrun growing-moment estimate.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  The axiom audit for
  the new public declaration reports only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Upper fixed-moment bulk almost-positivity now has dependent-index
  probability adapters:
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_bad_event_measure_le_of_centered_moment_threshold_event_measure_le_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:372` and
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_tendsto_bad_event_measure_zero_of_centered_moment_threshold_event_tendsto_zero_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:456`.
- Challenge Before:
  the fixed-moment bulk route already had deterministic count and trace-mass
  bridges, plus a fixed-index measure adapter.  A concrete spectrum indexed by
  a dimension-dependent type `ι d` still needed manual transport from a
  threshold-event concentration estimate to the bad event where either the
  negative eigenvalue fraction or normalized negative trace mass exceeds `η`.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now chooses one fixed moment order from `λ > 4` and transfers
  dimensionwise or asymptotic bounds on the centered-moment threshold event
  directly to the combined almost-positivity bad event for spectra indexed by
  `ι d`.  This closes Level 1 fixed-moment probability plumbing.  It does not
  prove the fixed-order concentration estimate itself, and it does not imply
  full PPT; those remain separate theorem-strength inputs.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  Axiom audits for
  both new public declarations report only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

  the exponential background-concentration branch had a mixed-frontier

  background-concentration branch.  The remaining theorem-strength inputs are

  the exponential-tail branch had the direct one-`Q`/many-`Q` scalar split, and
