- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes; `lake env lean /tmp/check_lower_oneq_scale_exp.lean` checks the
  public signature; the declaration audits to `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`; the touched Lean file contains no `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`,
  or `unsafe`.

- Lower survivor-core exponential branch now reduces both PT direct scalar
  mixed leaves to scale comparisons:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_noReferenceClosedTraceAndPTSurvivorCoreFrontierInputs_withPTScaleComparisons_splitMixedWordBudget_exponentialDeviationStack`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:2840`.
- Challenge Before:
  the exponential branch had the one-`Q` scale-comparison adapter, while the
  many-`Q` direct scalar trace bound still remained visible.  The variance
  branch already had the corresponding both-scale-comparisons endpoint.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now supplies the many-`Q` direct scalar bound internally from
  `lowerConcretePTMixedWordManyQScaleComparison R k ε M` and calls the one-`Q`
  scale endpoint.  The live lower inputs on this route are survivor analysis,
  the exponential deleted-background deviation theorem, nonnegative `M`, and
  the two PT scale comparisons.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes; `lake env lean /tmp/check_lower_scale_exp.lean` checks the public
  signature; the declaration audits to `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`; the touched Lean file contains no `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`,
  or `unsafe`.

- Lower survivor-core exponential branch now has a direct-scalar mixed endpoint:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_noReferenceClosedTraceAndPTSurvivorCoreFrontierInputs_withPTDirectMixedWordScalarCases_splitMixedWordBudget_exponentialDeviationStack`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:2771`.
- Challenge Before:
  the stronger exponential-tail branch had a pointwise PT mixed-word endpoint,
  but the direct one-`Q`/many-`Q` scalar split was available only on the
  variance-stack branch.  A caller using the exponential background input still
  had to package those two scalar leaves into a pointwise word estimate first.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now supplies the pointwise PT word estimate internally from
  `lowerConcretePTMixedWordOneQDirectScalarBound R k ε M` and
  `lowerConcretePTMixedWordManyQDirectScalarBound R k ε M`, then calls the
  exponential-tail endpoint.  The live lower inputs on this branch are
  survivor analysis, the exponential deleted-background deviation theorem, and
  those two direct scalar mixed trace bounds.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes.  The public declaration audits to `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Lower no-reference runtime-native diagnostic now sits at the preferred
  survivor/variance endpoint level:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_noReferenceClosedTraceAndPTSurvivorCoreFrontierInputs_withRuntimeMixedError_varianceStack`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3823`
  proves the no-reference lower conclusion from survivor analysis, the
  variance/Chebyshev source, and the packaged smallness of the exact
  runtime-native mixed error.  The companion
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError_three_not_errorEventuallySmall`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3465`
  refutes that packaged runtime-smallness predicate at `k = 3`.
- Challenge Before:
  the deterministic runtime-native mixed envelope was closed, and separate
  diagnostics refuted its uniform smallness at length three, but the preferred
  no-reference survivor/variance endpoint still displayed the mixed side mainly
  as an abstract `lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier`.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now has the no-reference survivor/variance endpoint with the exact
  runtime-native mixed error exposed.  The deterministic envelope and budget are
  supplied internally by `lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier_of_runtimeWordError`;
  the only remaining mixed input on this diagnostic branch is
  `lowerConcreteMixedErrorEventuallySmall k ε
  (lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError R k)`.  At `k = 3`, the new companion
  theorem proves that this input is impossible, so this branch is formally
  classified as diagnostic rather than an unconditional lower route.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes.  Axiom audits for both new public declarations report only
  `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

- Lower fixed-`M` scale-comparison diagnostic closed:
  `AppendixB.lowerConcretePTMixedWordOneQScaleComparison_three_not_uniform` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:5936`
  proves that the length-three one-`Q` scale-comparison hypothesis cannot hold.
  The packet-level companion
  `AppendixB.lowerConcretePTMixedWordScaleComparisons_three_not_uniform` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:5987`
  proves that the full length-three fixed-`M` scale-comparison packet
  `hOneScale ∧ hManyScale` is impossible.
- Challenge Before:
  the fixed-`M` scale-comparison branch still had a formal theorem accepting
  `lowerConcretePTMixedWordOneQScaleComparison R k ε M`; although the runtime
  domination route was known dead, neither the scale-comparison hypothesis
  itself nor the full conjunction consumed by the public endpoint had been
  explicitly ruled out at a concrete length.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now shows that at `k = 3` this one-`Q` scale comparison would imply the
  distinguished `QAA` runtime word is eventually dominated by the literal
  fixed-`M` PT word budget, contradicting the existing
  `lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordBound_headQRestA_two_not_eventually_le_PT`
  obstruction.  Since the public scale-comparison packet includes this
  one-`Q` component, Lean also refutes the full packet.  This keeps the lower
  frontier honest: the fixed-`M` scale-comparison branch is diagnostic only,
  not a viable unconditional route.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  passes.  The axiom audits for the new public theorems report only `[propext,
  Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

- Upper direct growing-moment convergence bridge now has a coordinate
  a.e. measurability endpoint:
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_lintegral_tendsto_zero_dependent_of_coordinate`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:1306`.
- Challenge Before:
  the cleanest direct upper endpoint consumed convergence to zero of the
  centered-moment `lintegral`, but still required a broad a.e. measurability
  hypothesis for the whole centered even-moment integrand.
- Challenge After:
  the direct endpoint now has a coordinate-measurability wrapper.  If a future
  concrete growing-moment theorem is stated directly as `lintegral → 0` over a
  dimension-dependent spectrum, coordinate a.e. measurability plus that theorem
  is enough for Lean to prove convergence to zero of the non-PPT event
  probability.  The hard input remains the concrete growing-moment convergence
  theorem.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  The axiom audit for
  the new public endpoint reports only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Upper controlled growing-moment route now has a coordinate-measurability
  pair of endpoints:
  `AubrunAlternative.centered_even_moment_lintegrand_aemeasurable_of_coordinate`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:480` and
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_log_order_constants_and_tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_four_lt_eventually_dependent_of_coordinate`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:1138`, plus the finite-rate
  companion
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_log_order_constants_and_eventually_negative_event_measure_le_of_four_lt_dependent_of_coordinate`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:1225`.
- Challenge Before:
  the active dependent-index upper scalar packages still required a broad
  a.e. measurability hypothesis for the whole finite centered-moment
  `lintegrand`; after the first coordinate wrapper, the finite-rate package
  still had the broad hypothesis.
- Challenge After:
  coordinate a.e. measurability of the spectral functions now supplies the
  centered even-moment `lintegrand` measurability internally for both the
  convergence-to-zero and finite-rate upper packages.  The remaining
  theorem-strength input is unchanged: the eventual growing-moment bound for
  the concrete partial-transpose spectrum.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  Axiom audits for
  the new public declarations report only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Upper polynomial-rate high-moment bridges now support dimension-dependent
  spectral index types:
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_lintegral_bound_const_mul_rpow_neg_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:734` and
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_lintegral_bound_log_rpow_mul_rpow_neg_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:827`.
- Challenge Before:
  the simple polynomial and log-polynomial upper probability bridges were
  fixed-index, so a future concrete moment estimate of the form `C*d^{-β}` or
  `C*(log d)^α*d^{-β}` over spectra indexed by `ι d` still required manual
  dependent-index transport.
- Challenge After:
  those rate forms now have dependent-index endpoints.  If a future
  growing-moment theorem gives either polynomial or log-polynomial decay for
  the concrete spectrum, Lean directly obtains convergence to zero of the
  non-PPT event probability.  The theorem-strength input remains the actual
  moment estimate.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  Axiom audits for
  both new public endpoints report only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Upper direct growing-moment convergence bridge now supports
  dimension-dependent spectral index types:
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_lintegral_tendsto_zero_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:1277`.
- Challenge Before:
  the cleanest abstract upper endpoint,
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_lintegral_tendsto_zero`,
  was fixed-index.  A concrete growing-moment theorem stated directly as
  convergence to zero of the centered-moment `lintegral` over an index type
  `ι d` still needed a dependent-index adapter.
- Challenge After:
  the dependent direct convergence adapter is closed.  If a future
  random-matrix theorem proves the unnormalised centered even moment
  `lintegral` tends to zero for the concrete spectrum indexed by `ι d`, Lean
  now immediately obtains convergence to zero of the non-PPT event
  probability.  This is still only the Markov/event-inclusion bridge; it does
  not prove the growing-moment theorem itself.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  The axiom audit for
  the new public endpoint reports only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Upper controlled growing-moment route now has fully scalar `λ > 4`
  packaging for dimension-dependent spectral index types:
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_log_order_constants_and_tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_four_lt_eventually_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:1108` and
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_log_order_constants_and_eventually_negative_event_measure_le_of_four_lt_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:1192`.
- Challenge Before:
  the dependent-index upper asymptotic bridge was available, but the public
  scalar package that chooses `eps`, `q = (4 + eps) / λ`, and `c` from
  `λ > 4` was still fixed-index.  A concrete spectrum with index type `ι d`
  still needed manual constant-choice plumbing.
- Challenge After:
  the scalar constant-choice wrapper is now dependent-index as well.  A future
  concrete growing-moment supplier can state the eventual paper-shape
  `lintegral` bound directly over `ι d`; Lean then chooses the constants and
  proves either probability convergence to zero or the eventual finite-rate
  bound.  The growing-moment/operator-norm estimate itself remains the hard
  theorem-strength input.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  Axiom audits for
  both new public endpoints report only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Upper controlled growing-moment bridge now supports dimension-dependent
  spectral index types in the asymptotic endpoint:
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_eventually_lintegral_bound_log_quadratic_rpow_log_dependent_of_two_lt_mul_log_inv`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:969`.
- Challenge Before:
  the upper high-moment/Aubrun-adjacent probability bridge had fixed-index
  asymptotic wrappers and a dependent-index finite-rate wrapper, but concrete
  matrix spectra usually use an index type depending on `d`.  Future
  random-matrix suppliers would still need an adapter from the dependent
  eventual paper-shape moment estimate to the actual non-PPT probability
  tending to zero.
- Challenge After:
  the dependent-index asymptotic adapter is closed.  The new bridge first
  packages the general real-rate form
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_eventually_lintegral_bound_ofReal_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:653`, then the paper-shape
  log-quadratic rate
  `AubrunAlternative.tendsto_negative_event_measure_zero_of_eventually_lintegral_bound_log_quadratic_rpow_log_dependent`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:913`, and finally the readable
  condition `2 < c * log(q⁻¹)`.  This removes a concrete-model plumbing gap
  but still does not prove the growing-moment/operator-norm estimate.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  Axiom audits for
  all three new public adapters report only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`.

- Literal PT mixed-error frontier now has a direct scalar-case supplier:
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier_of_PTDirectScalarCases` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3319`
  proves the same PT `lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier` from the direct
  one-`Q` and many-`Q` scalar trace bounds
  `lowerConcretePTMixedWordOneQDirectScalarBound R k ε M` and
  `lowerConcretePTMixedWordManyQDirectScalarBound R k ε M`.
- Challenge Before:
  the literal PT mixed frontier was supplied from a pointwise word estimate,
  while the known finite split from direct scalar leaves to pointwise PT words
  lived one layer lower in `LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean`.
- Challenge After:
  the canonical mixed-frontier supplier can now skip the pointwise-word
  packaging hypothesis when the two direct scalar leaves are available.  The
  remaining mixed theorem-strength inputs on this branch are exactly those two
  scalar trace bounds; the finite word split, PT coefficient budget, and PT
  scalar smallness are adapter-supplied.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes.  The axiom audit for the new public adapter reports only `[propext,
  Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

- Literal PT mixed-error frontier now has a named pointwise-to-frontier
  supplier:
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier_of_PTPointwiseWordBound` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3287`
  proves `lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier R k ε
  (fun a slack d => lowerPartialTransposeMixedErrorD k (a + slack) M d)` from
  the pointwise literal PT word estimate
  `lowerConcreteMixedWordPointwiseBoundOnSphere R k ε
  (fun a slack d => lowerPartialTransposeMixedWordBoundD k (a + slack) M d)`,
  together with `3 ≤ k`, `0 < ε`, and `0 ≤ M`.
- Challenge Before:
  the honest mixed-frontier constructor reduced the mixed side to pointwise
  word bounds, a finite budget, and scalar smallness, but on the literal PT
  branch the finite budget and `o(1)` smallness still had to be reconstructed
  inline by callers.
- Challenge After:
  the PT finite budget and scalar smallness are adapter-supplied.  The scalar
  smallness is named separately as
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedErrorEventuallySmall_of_lowerPartialTransposeMixedErrorD`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3272`.
  The remaining mixed theorem-strength input on this PT branch is the
  pointwise word estimate on the Frobenius sphere; this does not prove the
  pointwise estimate and does not revive the fixed-`M` runtime-domination
  branch.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes.  Axiom audits for both new public adapters report only `[propext,
  Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

- Lower no-reference route is tracked at the honest mixed-frontier level:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_noReferenceClosedTraceAndPTSurvivorCoreFrontierInputs_withMixedErrorFrontier_varianceStack`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3620`
  proves the lower endpoint from the geodesic/noncrossing survivor analysis,
  the paper-facing variance/Chebyshev source, and one matched
  `lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier R k ε errMix`.
- Challenge Before:
  the status ledger was still treating the fixed-`M` PT scale-comparison branch
  as the preferred lower no-reference route.  That branch is a useful
  conditional diagnostic, but it is not the honest unconditional path because
  the runtime threshold `lowerConcreteM R a slack d` is dimension-dependent and
  fixed-`M` runtime domination has explicit negative diagnostics.
- Challenge After:
  the preferred lower route is the mixed-error frontier.  The frontier itself
  is no longer opaque: `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedErrorFrontier_of_wordBoundsAndBudget`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3252`
  supplies it from `lowerConcreteMixedWordPointwiseBoundOnSphere R k ε bound`,
  `lowerConcreteMixedWordBudgetWithError R k ε bound errMix`, and
  `lowerConcreteMixedErrorEventuallySmall k ε errMix`.  The remaining
  theorem-strength inputs on the preferred branch are survivor analysis, the
  variance/Chebyshev theorem, and these three mixed-frontier components unless
  a no-input supplier is proved later.  The fixed-`M` PT scale-comparison
  endpoint remains available as a compatibility/diagnostic branch, not as the
  atlas-preferred lower route.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  passes, and
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes.  The axiom audits for the mixed-frontier adapter, the new many-`Q`
  adapter, and the public endpoints report only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`; the touched Lean files contain no active `sorry`, `admit`,
  `axiom`, or `unsafe`.

- Previous no-reference mixed-word/budget adapter:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_noReferenceClosedTraceAndPTSurvivorCoreFrontierInputs_withMixedWordBoundsAndBudget_varianceStack`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3696`
  proves the lower endpoint from the geodesic/noncrossing survivor analysis,
  sphere-supported word-by-word mixed bounds, a finite scalar budget into
  `errMix`, eventual smallness of that `errMix`, and the paper-facing
  deleted-background variance/Chebyshev source.

- Closed-form sample-ratio lower route exposed with variance and packaged

  deleted-column moment limit, the variance/Chebyshev theorem, and
  `mixed_noL_atLeastTwoQ_ge_neg_errMix R k ε M`.
- Challenge Before:
  the sample-ratio closed-form fixed-`M` PT mixed branch still exposed the
  internal grouped second-moment Wick/Chebyshev tail via the older
  `SecondMomentWickStack` wrapper, even though the pointwise PT sibling and the
  abstract mixed-frontier sibling already used the paper-facing variance
  theorem.
- Challenge After:
  the packaged fixed-`M` PT mixed branch now has the same paper-facing
  variance/Chebyshev input as the pointwise and mixed-frontier branches.  The
  remaining theorem-strength inputs on this branch are the sample-ratio
  closed-form/Hankel mean theorem, the variance/Chebyshev theorem, and the
  packaged fixed-`M` PT mixed supplier.  The pointwise PT branch remains the
  sharper route when the actual sphere-supported word estimate is available.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  passes.  The axiom audit for the new public theorem reports only `[propext,
  Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; the touched Lean file contains no active

- Closed-form sample-ratio lower route exposed at the honest mixed-frontier

  proves the lower endpoint from the sample-ratio closed-form/Hankel
  deleted-column moment limit, the variance/Chebyshev theorem, and an explicit

- Challenge Before:
  the sample-ratio closed-form branch had a pointwise PT public endpoint, but
  the more primitive mixed-frontier endpoint was only available in the ratio
  convention.  Callers using sample-ratio closed-form input still had to route
  through an internal ratio-convention adapter before choosing a mixed error.
- Challenge After:
  the sample-ratio branch now has a public mixed-frontier endpoint.  The
  remaining theorem-strength inputs on this branch are
  `lowerDeletedColumnBackgroundMomentHasClosedFormMomentLimit_fromSampleRatio
  R.sample k`, `deletedColumnSphericalMoment_variance_le_const_div_d4 R k`,

  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`

- Closed-form sample-ratio lower route exposed with exponential background

- Challenge Before:
  the sample-ratio closed-form pointwise route still displayed the internal
  grouped second-moment Wick/Chebyshev tail rather than the paper-facing

- Challenge After:
  the sample-ratio closed-form branch now has a public endpoint with the

- Challenge Before:
  the closed-form mean branch had a direct variance-plus-abstract-mixed
  endpoint and a separate sample-ratio pointwise endpoint, but no direct

- Challenge After:
  the closed-form branch can now consume the pointwise PT mixed-word supplier

  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`

- Lower Catalan-error/variance route reduced to runtime scalar domination:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_CatalanErrorBoundAndVarianceStack_withPTRuntimeMixedScalarDomination_splitMixedWordBudget`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:3654`
  proves the lower endpoint from the concrete deleted-column `D / d`
  Catalan-error estimate, the variance/Chebyshev theorem, and two explicit
  eventual scalar comparisons between the runtime mixed envelope and the
  literal PT mixed envelope.
- Challenge Before:
  the scalar runtime-domination route still used the broader

- Lower runtime-smallness public-input obstruction closed:
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError_three_not_uniformEventuallySmall`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:5338`
  proves that the exact uniform runtime-smallness hypothesis appearing in the
  runtime-native length-three lower wrapper is impossible.  The comments on
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_paperFacingPTCatalanMeanAndVarianceStack_withPTRuntimeMixedError_splitMixedWordBudget`
  and `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_corrected`
  now explicitly classify that route as conditional/diagnostic, not as an
  unconditional paper-facing supplier.
- Challenge Before:
  the scalar `QAA` obstruction showed that each fixed admissible `a, slack`
  cannot make `lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError R 3 a slack` arbitrarily
  small, but the exact public quantifier shape of the runtime-smallness input
  was not separately named; two endpoint comments could still be read as if
  this runtime-native smallness were the honest final mixed supplier.
- Challenge After:
  the rejected runtime branch is now blocked in the same quantifier shape as
  the public wrapper.  The live lower theorem-strength mixed leaf is therefore
  the paper-facing PT mixed frontier, not runtime-native `o(1)` smallness and
  not fixed-`M` runtime-to-PT domination.  This is progress toward an
  unconditional lower proof by removing a false candidate route from the
  active frontier.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  and `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`
  pass.  The axiom audit for the new public theorem reports only `[propext,
  Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; the touched-file scan found no active
  `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`.

- Upper fixed-moment bad-event measure adapter closed:
  `AubrunAlternative.exists_bad_event_measure_le_of_centered_moment_threshold_event_measure_le`
  at `PptFactorization/AubrunAlternative.lean:348` proves the measure-level
  version of the already audited fixed-moment bulk inclusion.  For every
  `lambda > 4` and `eta > 0`, Lean chooses a fixed moment order `m`; any bound
  on the Catalan-plus-half-tolerance centered-moment threshold event transfers
  directly to the event that either the normalized negative count or normalized
  negative trace mass exceeds `eta`.
- Challenge Before:
  the deterministic upper bulk route had the set inclusion
  `exists_bad_event_subset_centered_moment_threshold_event`, but a
  probability/concentration supplier still had to manually apply measure
  monotonicity to use it.
- Challenge After:
  the fixed-moment adapter layer now exposes the probability-facing theorem
  directly, with no extra random-matrix or growing-moment hypothesis hidden in
  the statement.  This supports the rock-solid article claim that fixed moments
  give quantitative bulk almost-positivity once the corresponding fixed-moment
  threshold event is controlled.  It still does not prove full PPT or Aubrun's
  spectral-edge theorem; those remain the controlled growing-moment/operator
  norm frontier.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AubrunAlternative` passes.  The axiom audit for
  the new public theorem reports only `[propext, Classical.choice,
  Quot.sound]`; the touched Lean file contains no active `sorry`, `admit`,
  `axiom`, or `unsafe`.

- Lower length-three runtime non-smallness diagnostic closed:
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordBound_headQRestA_two_eventually_ge`
  at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:4993`
  proves that the distinguished word `QAA` has an eventual scalar lower bound
  by
  `N(d)^2 * (a * N(d)^(-1 + 1/3) * (256 * (256 * sqrt N(d))))` in the
  endpoint spike window.  The new power-factor lemma
  `AppendixB.lower_headQRestA_two_powerFactor_ge_one` at line `5088` and the
  scalar constant lemma
  `AppendixB.lower_headQRestA_two_scalar_eventually_ge_const` at line `5113`
  show that this displayed scalar lower bound is eventually at least
  `256*256*a`.  The corollary
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError_three_eventually_ge_headQRestA_scalar`
  at line `5271` lifts the scalar lower bound to the full length-three runtime
  mixed error.  Finally,
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError_three_not_eventuallySmall` at
  line `5297` proves that this runtime-native error is not eventually
  arbitrarily small for any admissible `a` and positive `slack`.  The sharper
  diagnostic
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordBound_headQRestA_two_not_eventually_le_PT`
  at line `5338` proves that even the single `QAA` runtime word cannot be
  eventually dominated by any fixed-`M` literal PT word budget.
- Challenge Before:
  the proof graph had the named `QAA` witness and an extraction theorem from a
  one-word runtime lower bound to the full runtime error, plus an eventual
  scalar lower bound for that witness's envelope, but it still stopped short of
  an explicit contradiction to runtime `o(1)` smallness or to the fixed-`M` PT
  domination leaf.
- Challenge After:
  the runtime `QAA` route is closed as a negative diagnostic: the event-native
  runtime mixed error cannot be the paper-facing small error, and the fixed-`M`
  runtime-to-PT domination route is impossible already on one word.  The
  remaining lower theorem-strength leaf is not this scalar asymptotic anymore;
  it is the honest paper-facing mixed frontier tying an actual local-expansion
  envelope to an actually small `errMix`, as exposed by
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_paperFacingPTCatalanMeanAndVarianceStack_withMixedErrorFrontier_splitMixedWordBudget`.
  No claim of the final lower no-input theorem is made by this entry.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  and `lake build PptFactorization` pass.  Axiom audits for the four new
  public theorems report only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; the
  touched-file scan found no active `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`;
  `git diff --check` is clean for the touched files.

- Lower one-word runtime witness closed:
  `AppendixB.lowerHeadQRestAWord` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:4765`
  defines the explicit word with a head `Q` and background `A` letters
  elsewhere.  `AppendixB.lowerHeadQRestAWord_Q_count`,
  `AppendixB.lowerHeadQRestAWord_L_count`, and
  `AppendixB.lowerHeadQRestAWord_mixed` at lines `4769`, `4776`, and `4788`
  prove it has exactly one `Q`, no `L`, and is mixed when its tail is nonempty.
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordBound_headQRestA_eq` at line `4970`
  gives the exact runtime envelope formula for this word, and
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError_eventually_ge_headQRestA` at
  line `5252` proves the full runtime error eventually dominates this word.
- Challenge Before:
  the extraction adapter showed that any one mixed word could witness
  non-smallness, but no canonical word was fixed in the proof graph and no exact
  one-word runtime formula was named.
- Challenge After:
  the canonical one-`Q` witness is fixed and audited.  The remaining lower
  mixed diagnostic is now a scalar asymptotic problem for the displayed formula
  of `lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordBound_headQRestA_eq`; the finite word
  construction, count facts, mixedness, and lift to the full runtime error are
  closed.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  and `lake build PptFactorization` pass.  Axiom audits for the five new public
  theorems report only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` or the smaller
  foundational subset `[propext, Quot.sound]`; the touched-file scan found no
  active `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`; `git diff --check` is clean
  for the touched files.

- Lower runtime mixed extraction adapter closed:
  `AppendixB.localMixedWordFilteredSum_nonneg` and
  `AppendixB.localMixedWordFilteredSum_single_le` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:4725`
  and `:4739` prove the generic finite filtered-sum facts needed to extract
  one mixed word from an error envelope.  The runtime specializations
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordBound_eventually_nonneg`,
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError_eventually_nonneg`, and
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordBound_le_runtimeWordError_eventually`
  at lines `4931`, `4981`, and `5004` prove that, in the endpoint spike
  window, the exact runtime mixed error is eventually nonnegative and
  eventually dominates every individual mixed runtime word.
- Challenge Before:
  the runtime coefficient was known to be scale-incompatible with a hidden
  fixed-`M` smallness argument, but the proof graph had no audited adapter
  allowing a future lower bound for one explicit mixed word to be lifted to the
  full runtime error.
- Challenge After:
  this finite-sum extraction layer is closed.  To rule out
  `lowerConcreteMixedErrorEventuallySmall k ε
  (lowerConcreteMixedRuntimeWordError R k)` itself, it now suffices to prove a
  lower bound for one chosen mixed word in the runtime envelope; the remaining
  work is theorem-strength scalar asymptotics for such a word, not filtered-sum
  bookkeeping.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  and `lake build PptFactorization` pass.  Axiom audits for the five new public
  theorems report only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; the
  touched-file scan found no active `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`;
  `git diff --check` is clean for the touched files.

- Lower fixed-scale diagnostic strengthened:
  `AppendixB.lower_concrete_sample_eventually_ge` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:3750`
  proves that the balanced concrete sample count eventually exceeds any fixed
  natural number, and
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteM_div_lowerConcreteN_eventually_ge_real` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:3798`
  proves that `lowerConcreteM R a slack d / lowerConcreteN d` eventually
  exceeds any fixed real bound.
- Challenge Before:
  Lean certified that the runtime background coefficient was not eventually
  arbitrarily small, but the exact strength of the obstruction was still only
  recorded as a fixed lower bound.
- Challenge After:
  the current runtime background coefficient is formally unbounded along every
  balanced regime.  Thus the present favourable-event scale cannot supply a
  fixed-`M` mixed-domination coefficient by a hidden smallness argument; a
  lower unconditional route must sharpen the background threshold or use a
  different mixed envelope.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  passes.  Axiom audits for both new public theorems report only
  `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; the touched-file scan found no
  active `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`.

- Public lower-frontier wiring:
  `PptFactorization.lean` now imports
  `PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices`, so the
  sharp lower concrete frontier and the runtime mixed diagnostic are part of
  the public project import chain.
- Challenge Before:
  the strongest lower frontier was build-checked as a standalone target but
  was not visible from the root module `PptFactorization`; importing only the
  older closure aliases could leave downstream users on stale conditional
  wrappers.
- Challenge After:
  `import PptFactorization` exposes the lower mean-limit frontier, its
  explicit-diagram runtime-mixed endpoint, and the
  `lowerConcreteM / lowerConcreteN` non-smallness diagnostic.  This is
  structural progress, not a new theorem-strength closure.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization` passes.

- Lower mixed-route diagnostic closed:
  `AppendixB.lowerConcreteM_div_lowerConcreteN_not_eventuallySmall` at
  `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices.lean:3752`
  proves that the current runtime background coefficient
  `lowerConcreteM R a slack d / lowerConcreteN d` is not eventually
  arbitrarily small.
- Challenge Before:
  the runtime-native mixed route still looked like a plausible direct
  `o(1)` target if one mentally treated the runtime background coefficient as
  a PT edge-scale scalar.
- Challenge After:
  Lean now certifies that this scalar coefficient is eventually at least
  `256`, hence not `o(1)`.  This does not disprove every possible mixed-error
  estimate, but it formally rules out the naive runtime-smallness route based
  on shrinking `lowerConcreteM / lowerConcreteN`.  The next lower proof move
  must either sharpen the favourable background threshold or replace the mixed
  estimate by a genuinely smaller envelope.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AristotleTargets.LowerMixedLowerConcreteChoices`
  passes.  Axiom audit for the new diagnostic theorem reports only
  `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`; the touched-file scan found no
  active `sorry`, `admit`, `axiom`, or `unsafe`; `git diff --check` is clean
  for the touched Lean file.

- Lower adapter closed:
  `AppendixB.lower_eventual_log_over_spikeSpeed_concreteModel_of_ExplicitCatalanDiagramAndVarianceStack_withPTRuntimeMixedError_splitMixedWordBudget`
  at `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerMeanLimitConcreteChoices.lean:4030`
  now routes the explicit finite-diagram Catalan mean frontier and the
  variance frontier through the runtime-native mixed envelope.
- Challenge Before:

  consumes `hLemma43AutoHeightBands` instead of raw cap comparison and still
  exposes the same below-half normalized surface-cap tail.  This is a reroute,
  not a theorem-strength closure: the Lemma 4.3 data supplier and the
  surface-cap tail remain the two hard geometric leaves on this branch.
- Verification:
  `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBUpperBoundClosure` passes; axiom audit
  for the new theorem reports only `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

## Full Spherical Isoperimetry Frontier

The reflection-map Jacobian / surface push-forward theorem is closed and now
imported by the project entrypoint:

```lean
PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRealSphereReflectionMap_pushforward_withDensity
PptFactorization.AppendixB.finRealSphereReflectionMap_isJacobianTarget
```

The global theorem `FullSphericalIsoperimetry` is not a consequence of that
single local theorem by itself.  It has now been decomposed in
`PptFactorization/AppendixBSurfaceMeasure.lean` into two explicit no-input
global suppliers:

```lean
sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparison
sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo
sphere_hemisphereGaussianTail
```

The hemisphere-tail supplier has also been sharpened into a geometric
coordinate-dominance step, a one-dimensional coordinate tail, and a closed
large-radius supplier:

```lean
sphere_hemisphereComplementCoordinateDominance
sphere_hemisphereComplementCoordinateDominance_surface
sphere_coordinateGaussianTail
sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent
sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_closedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_closedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_coordinateCosinePowerTailBelowHalf
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail_of_coordinateCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTail_of_closedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_capConeCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail_of_capConeCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_capConeCosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_capConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf
sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_cosinePowerTail
sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_cosinePowerTailBelowHalf
sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorLargeExponentNorthPole_of_coneTail
sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_coordinateTail
sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorLargeExponentNorthPole
sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorLargeExponent_of_northPole
sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorLargeExponent
sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorGeTwo_of_largeExponent
sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorGeTwo
sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInterior
sphere_coordinateGaussianTail_of_interior
sphere_hemisphereLargeRadiusTail_surface
sphere_hemisphereGaussianTail_of_coordinateDominance_and_coordinateTail
```

and the checked adapter is:

```lean
sphere_caps_minimize_neighborhoods_of_hemisphereComparison_and_tail
fullSphericalIsoperimetry_of_hemisphereComparison_and_tail
```

The direct auto-height-band block-to-`sSup` data now has its own public
cap-comparison closure point:
`sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_lemma43_autoHeightBands_directGainSup_equal_mass_pos_lt_pi`.
The sharp upper endpoint derives `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo`
through this theorem before assembling `FullSphericalIsoperimetry`.

Plain math meaning:

- `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparison`: every measurable half-mass set
  has geodesic-neighbourhood complement no larger than a closed hemisphere's
  corresponding complement.
- `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo`: the remaining half-measure
  cap-comparison theorem after the zero-dimensional sphere case is closed by
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparison_of_geTwo`.  In `S^0`, any
  half-mass set contains a point and that point is a closed hemisphere.  The
  first variational step for the `2 ≤ n` proof is now isolated by
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_near_complementInf`: for each radius
  and each positive tolerance there is a measurable half-mass competitor whose
  neighbourhood-complement mass is within that tolerance of the infimum.  The
  first polarization-admissibility bookkeeping step is also closed by
  `measurableSet_finRealSpherePolarization`: polarizing a measurable competitor
  again gives a measurable set.  The reflection inside the polarization is now
  identified with an ambient orthogonal linear isometry by
  `finRealSphereReflectionMap_eq_orthogonalSphereMap`; hence
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_map_reflection` and
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_reflection_preimage_real` give the
  measure-preserving ingredient for the mass-admissibility proof.  The full
  mass-admissibility bookkeeping is now closed as well:
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_polarization_real_eq` proves exact
  normalized surface-mass preservation under polarization, and
  `finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_polarization` proves that polarizing a
  measurable half-mass competitor again gives a half-mass competitor.  The
  reflection is also now certified as a geodesic isometry by
  `finRealSphereReflectionMap_geodesicDistance`, with the neighbourhood
  transport identity
  `finRealSphereReflectionMap_image_geodesicThickening`.  The pure-reflection
  objective step is now closed too:
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_reflection_image_neighbourhoodComplement_real`
  and `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_reflection_image` prove that
  replacing a competitor by its reflected image leaves the
  geodesic-neighbourhood complement mass unchanged.  The first pairwise metric
  inequalities for the two-point comparison are also closed:
  `finRealSphereReflectionMap_geodesicDistance_reflection_right_le_of_nonneg_nonpos`
  says reflecting a negative-side point toward a positive-side point does not
  increase geodesic distance, and
  `finRealSphereReflectionMap_geodesicDistance_le_reflection_left_of_nonneg_nonneg`
  says reflecting a positive-side point away from another positive-side point
  cannot shorten the distance.  The positive-side half of the
  polarization-neighbourhood inclusion is now closed as
  `finRealSpherePolarization_positiveSide_geodesicThickening_subset`: a point
  on the positive side that belongs to the polarized neighbourhood of `A`
  already belongs to the neighbourhood of the polarized set.  The two-point
  representative statement is now closed too:
  `finRealSpherePolarization_geodesicThickening_pair_of_mem` proves that every
  point of `N_r(A)` has at least one point in its reflection pair lying in
  `N_r(P_v A)`.  This now has two compiled measure-facing forms:
  `finRealSphereGeodesicThickening_subset_polarized_union_reflection_preimage`
  packages the pair statement as an honest set inclusion, and
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_positiveSide_pairNeighbourhood_real_le_polarizedThickening`
  turns the positive-side half into a `measureReal` inequality.  The generic
  reflection-invariant counting estimate
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_reflectionInvariant_real_le_two_positiveSide`
  is now closed as well: any reflection-invariant measurable set has total
  mass at most twice the mass of its positive-side representatives.  The
  composed neighbourhood version
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_pairNeighbourhoodUnion_real_le_two_polarizedThickening`
  is now closed too: the union `N_r(A) ∪ ρ_v^{-1} N_r(A)` has mass at most
  twice the mass of `N_r(P_v A)`.  The sharper no-factor-loss route is now
  closed as well: `finRealSphereGeodesicThickening_polarization_subset_polarization_geodesicThickening`
  proves `N_r(P_v A) ⊆ P_v(N_r A)`, and
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_geodesicThickening_polarization_real_le`
  plus `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_polarization_ge` turn this
  into the variational objective statement that polarization cannot decrease
  the geodesic-neighbourhood complement mass.  The broader orthogonal transport
  package `finRealOrthogonalSphereMap_image_geodesicThickening`,
  `finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure_orthogonal_image_neighbourhoodComplement_real`,
  and `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_orthogonal_image` is also now
  closed, so future hemisphere-normalization steps can rotate competitors
  without changing the objective.  The closed-hemisphere normalization package
  `finRealOrthogonalSphereMap_image_closedHemisphere`,
  `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_closedHemisphere_eq`, and
  `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_closedHemisphere_eq_northPole` is
  closed as well: all closed hemispheres have the same fixed-radius
  geodesic-neighbourhood complement objective, and downstream arguments may
  normalize the pole to the north pole.  The normalized supremum adapter
  `finRealSphere_complementSup_le_some_closedHemisphere_iff_northPole`,
  `finRealSphereHalfMeasureHemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_complementSup_le_northPole`,
  and `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_complementSup_le_northPole`
  is now closed too: the public dimension-at-least-two comparison is reduced
  to proving the north-pole hemisphere dominates the half-mass complement
  supremum at each radius.  The nonpositive-radius endpoint package
  `finRealSphereGeodesicThickening_eq_empty_of_nonpos`,
  `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_eq_one_of_nonpos`,
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_nonpos`, and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_complementSup_le_northPole_pos`
  is closed as well: since the project uses open thickenings, every
  nonpositive-radius thickening is empty and the complement objective is
  exactly `1`.  Thus the public cap-comparison theorem is reduced to the
  normalized north-pole supremum domination for positive radii only.  The
  large-radius endpoint package `finRealSphereGeodesicDistance_le_pi`,
  `FinRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor.nonempty`,
  `finRealSphereGeodesicThickening_eq_univ_of_pi_lt`,
  `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_eq_zero_of_pi_lt`,
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_pi_lt`, and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_complementSup_le_northPole_pos_le_pi`
  is also closed: for `π < r`, every nonempty half-mass competitor thickens to
  the whole sphere, so the complement objective is zero.  Thus the remaining
  strict-improvement/cap-characterization theorem only has to prove normalized
  north-pole supremum domination on the compact positive-radius interval
  `0 < r ≤ π`.  The diameter-boundary package
  `finRealSphere_dist_lt_two_of_ne_neg`,
  `finRealSphereGeodesicDistance_lt_pi_of_ne_neg`,
  `FinRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor.not_subset_singleton_surface`,
  `finRealSphereGeodesicThickening_eq_univ_of_pi_of_halfMass`,
  `finRealSphereNeighbourhoodComplementMass_eq_zero_of_pi_of_halfMass`,
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_pi`, and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_complementSup_le_northPole_pos_lt_pi`
  is now closed as well: at `r = π`, any half-mass competitor in dimension at
  least two contains a point not antipodal to each queried point, so its open
  `π`-neighbourhood is already the whole sphere and the complement objective is
  zero.  Thus the remaining strict-improvement/cap-characterization theorem
  only has to prove normalized north-pole supremum domination on the strict
  interval `0 < r < π`.  The correctly oriented
  maximizing plumbing around this objective is now available:
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup`, `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementValues_bddAbove_surface`,
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_ge_of_competitor`, and
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_near_complementSup` define and
  control the supremum of complement mass values.  The polarized
  maximizing-sequence adapter
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_near_complementSup_with_polarized_objective_ge`
  packages near-supremizers together with their polarized admissible no-worse
  competitors, and
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_strictImprovement_lt_tolerance` records
  the contradiction arithmetic: a `δ`-near supremizer cannot have an
  admissible improvement of size at least `η` unless `η < δ`.  The adapter
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_near_complementSup_no_admissible_eta_improvement`
  strengthens this from one fixed polarization to all admissible competitors:
  for every `η > 0`, there is a single `η / 2`-near supremizer that cannot be
  improved by `η` by any half-mass competitor.  Consequently,
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_near_complementSup_all_polarizations_no_eta_improvement`
  packages one common near-supremizer whose polarization in every direction is
  admissible, no worse, and still not an `η`-improvement.  The older adapter
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_near_complementSup_no_fixed_polarization_eta_improvement`
  remains as a fixed-direction compatibility form: for every
  `η > 0` and every polarization direction, there is an `η / 2`-near
  supremizer whose polarized competitor is admissible and no-worse, but cannot
  improve the objective by `η`.  The adapter
  `finRealSphereHalfMeasureHemisphereComparison_of_complementSup_le_hemisphere`,
  together with its dimension-at-least-two/no-input variants, now shows that it
  is enough to prove radius-wise domination of this complement supremum by the
  north-pole closed hemisphere for `0 < r < π`.  The order-theoretic final
  adapter
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_quantitative_gap_improvement`
  and its no-input wrapper
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_quantitative_gap_improvement_pos_lt_pi`
  now reduce that domination to a precise quantitative strict-improvement
  supplier: on `0 < r < π`, every half-mass competitor whose complement
  objective exceeds the north-pole hemisphere objective by `η > 0` must admit
  another half-mass competitor improving the objective by at least the same
  `η`.  The sharper adapter
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_polarization_gap_improvement`,
  with no-input endpoint
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_polarization_gap_improvement_pos_lt_pi`,
  specializes this to the canonical polarization route: it is enough to find a
  direction `v` whose polarization of the above-hemisphere competitor improves
  the complement objective by `η`, since polarization already preserves the
  half-mass competitor condition.  The still sharper uniform adapter
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_uniform_polarization_gap_improvement`,
  with no-input endpoint
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_uniform_polarization_gap_improvement_pos_lt_pi`,
  now allows the improvement size to be any positive `δ` depending only on the
  gap `η`, not necessarily `η` itself.  The helper
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_near_complementSup_no_admissible_delta_improvement`
  supplies the near-supremizer contradiction with an independent tolerance
  `β ≤ δ`, and
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_above_northPole_no_admissible_delta_improvement`
  now packages the exact contradiction object used by the uniform route: a
  half-mass competitor already at least `γ` above the north-pole objective while
  forbidding every admissible `δ`-improvement.  The strengthened helper
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_above_northPole_all_polarizations_no_delta_improvement`
  additionally packages, for every polarization direction at once, admissibility
  of the polarized competitor, the no-worse objective inequality, and the
  contradiction against a `δ`-improvement; the uniform polarization-gap theorem
  now uses this object directly.  The older `Inf` lemmas remain
  compiled bookkeeping, but they are not the canonical cap-comparison
  orientation.  The next live step is the genuine polarization strict-improvement
  supplier: above-hemisphere competitors on `0 < r < π` must admit a
  polarization direction improving the objective by some uniform positive
  amount determined by the gap.  This has now been sharpened one layer further:
  `finRealSpherePolarizationObjectiveGainValues` records the real objective
  gains over all polarization directions, and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_uniform_polarization_gainSup_lower_pos_lt_pi`
  shows it is enough to give a positive lower bound on the `sSup` of these
  gains.  The remaining theorem-strength leaf is therefore the Lemma 4.3
  average/supremum bridge in geometric form: from a positive hemisphere gap,
  prove that the supremum of polarization objective gains is bounded below by a
  positive constant depending only on that gap.  The helper
  `exists_finRealSphereHalfMassCompetitor_above_northPole_no_gainSup_lower`
  now packages the maximizing-sequence contradiction directly at the
  gain-supremum level: any uniform positive `sSup` lower bound contradicts the
  above-gap near-supremizer.  The adapter
  `finRealSpherePolarization_gainSup_lower_of_lemma43_measure_trimming` now
  instantiates the already-closed measure-trimming Lemma 4.3 theorem with
  `supDelta = sSup` of the real polarization gains, and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_lemma43_measure_trimming_gainSup_pos_lt_pi`
  shows that the only remaining geometric supplier is the concrete data for
  Lemma 4.3: cap model `C`, trimmed bands, balanced symmetric difference,
  rectangular block lower bound, and the average-to-gain-supremum comparison.
  The direct-gain-supremum adapter later removes the auxiliary `avg` from the
  active theorem-facing form by setting the rectangular-block target to
  `sSup` of the polarization objective gains itself.
  This round sharpened the balance requirement: the new equal-mass route
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_lemma43_measure_trimming_gainSup_equal_mass_pos_lt_pi`
  proves the balance automatically from
  `(finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure n).real C =
    (finRealSurfaceProbabilityMeasure n).real A`, via
  `finRealPolarization_balance_of_measureReal_eq`.  The live geometric data
  supplier may therefore provide a same-mass cap/model `C` instead of proving
  equality of `μ(C \ A)` and `μ(A \ C)` directly.  The next adapter
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_lemma43_heightBands_gainSup_equal_mass_pos_lt_pi`
  specializes the abstract trimmed bands to the concrete height bands
  `finRealSphereHeightBandAbove/Below`, so their measurability is no longer a
  live obligation.  The remaining supplier now has to choose `C`, a height pole
  and level, and prove same mass, far symmetric difference, the two band-mass
  bounds, and a rectangular block lower bound against the gain supremum.
  The band-mass existence step itself is now closed by
  `exists_finRealSphereHeightBands_small`: atomlessness of the height
  distribution implies that for every pole, level, and `eps > 0` there is a
  positive height thickness whose upper and lower bands both have surface mass
  at most `eps / 4`.  This has now been sharpened by
  `exists_finRealSphereHeightBands_small_le`, using the monotonicity lemmas
  `finRealSphereHeightBandAbove_subset_of_le` and
  `finRealSphereHeightBandBelow_subset_of_le`: the thin height thickness can
  also be chosen below any prescribed positive `tauMax`.  What remains is the
  genuinely global synchronization: produce, uniformly over the above-gap
  competitors, a same-mass model `C`, a pole/level, far symmetric difference,
  and a single rectangular block-to-supremum package compatible with the chosen
  small height thickness.  The rectangular-block side now also has
  the monotonicity adapter
  `SphericalPolarization.GeometricKernel.HasRectangularBlockLowerBound.mono_tau`:
  a block proved at a larger separation may be degraded to any smaller
  separation, as long as the two defect masses are nonnegative.  The concrete
  trimming dictionary now packages that nonnegativity too:
  `finRealPolarizationMuPlus_nonneg`,
  `finRealPolarizationMuMinus_nonneg`, and
  `finRealPolarization_rectangularBlockLowerBound_mono_tau` give the
  same degradation directly for the concrete trimmed defects.  The concrete
  trimming lower-bound step is also now public and audited:
  `finRealPolarization_trimmed_masses_lower_bound_of_balance` turns balanced
  symmetric-difference separation plus two small removed bands into
  `μ(D₊), μ(D₋) ≥ ε / 4`, while
  `finRealPolarization_trimmed_masses_lower_bound_of_equal_mass` derives the
  balance condition automatically from equal normalized surface mass of the
  model and competitor.  The automatic height-band route
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_lemma43_autoHeightBands_gainSup_equal_mass`
  and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_lemma43_autoHeightBands_gainSup_equal_mass_pos_lt_pi`
  now choose the concrete band thickness internally from height atomlessness:
  the remaining supplier only has to provide a positive `tauMax` such that the
  rectangular block lower bound targets `sSup` of the objective gains for every
  `0 < tauBand ≤ tauMax`.  The sharp direct-gain upper endpoint is wired to this
  auto-band supplier shape by
  `upper_eventual_from_concrete_sequences_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_oneSidedPositive_localExpansionMomentWordEnvelope_caseMixedWordBounds_caseMixedTermLimits_canonicalOneQTerm`;
  the coordinate-law direct-gain endpoint remains as a compatibility theorem
  at
  `upper_eventual_from_concrete_sequences_of_lemma43AutoHeightBandsDirectGainSup_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_oneSidedPositive_localExpansionMomentWordEnvelope_caseMixedWordBounds_caseMixedTermLimits_canonicalOneQTerm`;
  the older surface-cap compatibility endpoint is
  `upper_eventual_from_concrete_sequences_of_lemma43AutoHeightBands_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail_oneSidedPositive_localExpansionMomentWordEnvelope_caseMixedWordBounds_caseMixedTermLimits_canonicalOneQTerm`;
  the cap-cone power law, Gaussian cap-cone tail, and normalized Gaussian
  closed-halfspace tail are now internal consequences of the normalized
  cosine-power spherical-cap tail.  The remaining below-half tail has also
  been rerouted into the surface-cap form
  `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`, equivalent by the
  checked coordinate push-forward adapters, so the next proof can work directly
  with normalized surface measure of a north-pole cap.
  The older
  height-band route has therefore been relaxed by
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_lemma43_heightBands_rectTau_gainSup_equal_mass`
  and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_lemma43_heightBands_rectTau_gainSup_equal_mass_pos_lt_pi`:
  the future geometric supplier may now choose a small band thickness `tau`
  and prove the rectangular block at any larger separation `tauRect`.  The
  sharper active route is now
  `finRealSphereHalfMassComplementSup_le_northPole_of_lemma43_heightBands_separateTau_gainSup_equal_mass`
  and
  `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_of_lemma43_heightBands_separateTau_gainSup_equal_mass_pos_lt_pi`:
  it treats the height-band thickness `tauBand` and the rectangular
  kernel-separation `tauSep` as independent parameters.  This is the canonical
  frontier for the next round, because the remaining global geometric supplier
  no longer has to synchronize the trimming band scale with the kernel block
  scale.
- `sphere_hemisphereComplementCoordinateDominance`: the complement of a
  geodesically enlarged closed hemisphere is bounded by the opposite height
  coordinate tail at threshold `sin r` in the small-radius range
  `0 < r ≤ π/2`; the endpoint `r = 0` is discharged separately in
  `finRealSphereHemisphereGaussianTail_of_coordinateDominance_and_coordinateTail`
  by the probability bound `μ(E) ≤ 1`.  This supplier is closed by
  `sphere_hemisphereComplementCoordinateDominance_surface`.
- `sphere_coordinateGaussianTail`: that one-dimensional spherical coordinate
  tail is bounded by `exp(-((n - 1) r^2)/2)` in the same small-radius range.
  The endpoint cases `r = 0` and `r = π/2` are now closed by
  `sphere_coordinateGaussianTail_of_interior`: `r = 0` uses total probability
  at most one, while `r = π/2` uses containment of the opposite-coordinate tail
  at threshold `1` in the antipode singleton.
- `sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent`: the remaining
  strict-interior large-exponent north-pole tail is now connected in both
  directions with the spherical coordinate-law formulation, and the coordinate
  law is now connected directly to the normalized closed-halfspace surface
  statement.  The checked
  adapter `sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorLargeExponentNorthPole_of_coneTail`
  turns the ambient Haar cone-volume ratio into the north-pole spherical
  coordinate law, the checked adapter
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_coordinateTail` turns
  the coordinate law back into the cone-volume statement using the same
  `toSphere` cone formula, the checked adapter
  `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_coneTail`
  turns the ambient Haar cone-volume ratio directly into the normalized
  closed-halfspace surface-tail package, and the checked adapter
  `sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorLargeExponentNorthPole_of_closedHalfspaceTail`
  turns the normalized closed-halfspace inequality into the coordinate-law
  package.  The scalar comparison has also been split off:
  `real_cos_le_exp_neg_sq_div_two`,
  `real_cos_pow_nat_sub_one_le_exp_neg_mul_sq_div_two`, and
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_cosinePowerTail`
  turn the elementary cap-cone power law
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTail` into the large-exponent Gaussian
  package.  The checked adapter
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeGaussianTailLargeExponent_of_cosinePowerTailBelowHalf`
  now performs the same Gaussian-tail step from only the below-half cap-cone
  power law, because the large-exponent hypothesis implies the below-half
  condition.  The checked adapter
  `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail_of_coordinateCosinePowerTail`
  now turns the one-dimensional north-pole coordinate power tail
  `sphere_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTail` into the normalized
  spherical-cap power law.  The new checked adapter
  `sphere_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTail_of_belowHalf` closes the
  complementary range `1 / 2 ≤ cos(r)^(n-1)` by antipodal half-tail symmetry,
  so the active coordinate-tail supplier is only the nontrivial below-half
  package `sphere_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`.  The matching
  ambient cone-volume target is now also named directly as
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`, and
  `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_capConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`
  sends that exact below-half cone estimate into the active normalized
  surface-cap tail.  The
  modular upper adapter
  `upper_eventual_from_concrete_sequences_of_hemisphereComparison_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_oneSidedPositive_localExpansionMomentWordEnvelope_caseMixedWordBounds_caseMixedTermLimits_canonicalOneQTerm`
  consumes raw `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo` plus this
  below-half tail directly, so future cap-comparison work does not have to be
  routed through the auto-height-band Lemma 4.3 wrapper at the upper endpoint.
  The same route is now factored into two audited public adapters:
  `sphere_hemisphereGaussianTail_of_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`
  packages the entire below-half tail ladder into `sphere_hemisphereGaussianTail`,
  and
  `fullSphericalIsoperimetry_of_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_and_northPoleCoordinateCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`
  packages cap comparison plus that tail into `FullSphericalIsoperimetry`.
  The same below-half tail is now also available in the normalized surface-cap
  form `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`, with checked
  adapters in both directions via the coordinate push-forward formula, and the
  Lemma 4.3 surface-cap endpoint
  `upper_eventual_from_concrete_sequences_of_lemma43AutoHeightBands_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_oneSidedPositive_localExpansionMomentWordEnvelope_caseMixedWordBounds_caseMixedTermLimits_canonicalOneQTerm`
  is the preferred compiled target while cap comparison is still supplied by the
  auto-height-band route, and the independent surface-cap endpoint
  `upper_eventual_from_concrete_sequences_of_hemisphereComparison_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_oneSidedPositive_localExpansionMomentWordEnvelope_caseMixedWordBounds_caseMixedTermLimits_canonicalOneQTerm`
  is the preferred target once cap comparison is closed separately.
  The same independent route now also has a direct below-half cone-volume
  endpoint
  `upper_eventual_from_concrete_sequences_of_hemisphereComparison_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_oneSidedPositive_localExpansionMomentWordEnvelope_caseMixedWordBounds_caseMixedTermLimits_canonicalOneQTerm`,
  supplied by
  `fullSphericalIsoperimetry_of_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo_and_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTailBelowHalf`,
  so a future proof of the ambient cone-volume cosine-power law can feed the
  modular cap-comparison branch without first choosing the surface-cap endpoint
  by hand.
  The checked adapter
  `sphere_northPoleCapConeCosinePowerTail_of_closedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail`
  now turns the normalized spherical-cap power law
  `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail` into the ambient cap-cone
  power law by the surface/cone formula.  The reverse checked adapters
  `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTail_of_capConeCosinePowerTail`
  and
  `sphere_northPoleClosedHalfspaceCosinePowerTailBelowHalf_of_capConeCosinePowerTail`
  divide the cone estimate by the positive finite unit-ball volume, so an
  ambient cone-volume proof can now feed the active surface-cap below-half
  endpoint directly.  The next proof can therefore attack only the below-half
  one-dimensional coordinate-law inequality, equivalently the cone-volume
  power law with elementary target `cos(r)^(n-1)`, without separately proving
  the coordinate push-forward, surface-measure normalization step, or scalar
  exponential comparison.
  Orthogonal transitivity and surface-measure invariance still supply arbitrary
  poles through `sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorLargeExponent_of_northPole`;
  antipodal symmetry closes the small-exponent range
  `((n - 1) r^2) / 2 ≤ log 2` through
  `sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInteriorGeTwo_of_largeExponent`; and the `n = 1`
  case is discharged by probability boundedness in
  `sphere_coordinateGaussianTailInterior_of_geTwo`.
- `sphere_hemisphereLargeRadiusTail_surface`: proves `sphere_hemisphereLargeRadiusTail`;
  for `π/2 ≤ r`, the enlarged hemisphere complement is contained in the
  antipode singleton, hence has zero surface measure in dimension at least two
  and is trivially bounded in dimension one.

This is the active global-geometry frontier.  On the sharp upper branch, the
first item is now exposed in its auto-height-band Lemma 4.3 form
`hLemma43AutoHeightBands`, rather than as broad `FullSphericalIsoperimetry`,
raw `sphere_halfMeasure_hemisphereComparisonGeTwo`, or a supplier that must
preselect the thin band masses.  This is where the closed polarization/Jacobian
theorem feeds the classical cap-minimization argument; the cap-minimization
leaf is dimension-at-least-two only.  The remaining open tail work can now be
attacked directly as the below-half normalized north-pole coordinate-law
inequality, equivalently the hard part of the ambient cone-volume inequality
for the open radial cone over the north-pole cap, for `2 ≤ n`,
`0 < r < π/2`, and `cos(r)^(n-1) < 1 / 2`.  The regime
`1 / 2 ≤ cos(r)^(n-1)` is no longer a theorem-strength tail leaf.

## Lower Frontier

These are the only five explicit assumptions still visible in the strongest
concrete lower endpoint.  Closing any one of them is real progress only if the
gap disappears from the endpoint signature or from the no-input assembly's
supplier list after local Lean verification.

### hReference

Name: `hReference`

Lean statement consumed by the endpoint:

```lean
∀ᶠ d in atTop,
  ∀ i₀ : BipIndex (Fin d) (Fin d),
    SurfaceReferenceProjectiveCapConeCoordinateFormula
      (BipIndex (Fin d) (Fin d)) i₀
```

Current target:

```lean
AppendixB.lower_referenceCone_BipIndex_Fin_eventually_concreteChoices
```

File: `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerReferenceConeConcreteChoices.lean`.

Plain math meaning: the normalized surface measure of a projective coordinate
cone has the reference cap formula in the concrete ambient space
`BipIndex (Fin d) (Fin d)`.

Already available lemmas: reference cone/cap machinery around
`SurfaceReferenceProjectiveCapConeCoordinateFormula`,
`SurfaceProjectiveCapConeCoordinateFormula.of_reference`,
`projectiveCapProbability_toFinite_toSphere_eq_cone_volume_ratio`, and
`projectiveConeCoordinateRatio_eq_pow`.

Pointwise reduction now isolated:

```lean
AppendixB.lower_surfaceReferenceProjectiveCapConeCoordinateFormula_pointwise
```

First missing cone-volume lemma:

```lean
theorem lower_projectiveCapCone_coordinateUnitVector_toReal_volume_eq_pow_mul_ball_toReal
    {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
    (i₀ : ι) {r : ℝ} (hr0 : 0 ≤ r) (hr1 : r < 1) :
    ENNReal.toReal
        ((MeasureTheory.volume : Measure (EuclideanSpace ℂ ι))
          (projectiveCapCone (ι := ι)
            (coordinateUnitVector (ι := ι) i₀) r)) =
      r ^ (2 * (Fintype.card ι - 1)) *
        ENNReal.toReal
          ((MeasureTheory.volume : Measure (EuclideanSpace ℂ ι))
            (Metric.ball (0 : EuclideanSpace ℂ ι) 1))
```

Expected proof route: prove the ambient Lebesgue cone-volume computation for
the coordinate cap.  The pointwise reference formula then follows from
`projectiveCapProbability_toFinite_toSphere_eq_cone_volume_ratio`, positivity
and finiteness of the unit ball volume, and
`projectiveConeCoordinateRatio_eq_pow`.

Structural progress: high.  Mathematical hardness: medium-low.

Done criterion: the target compiles without `sorry`; the lower endpoint wrapper
uses it directly; `lake build PptFactorization.AppendixBLowerBoundClosure`
passes; endpoint axiom audit reports only `[propext, Classical.choice,
Quot.sound]`; `hReference` is no longer visible.

### hUnitProfile

Name: `hUnitProfile`

Lean statement consumed by the endpoint:

```lean
∀ a : ℝ, spikeRoot k ε < a →
  ∀ slack : ℝ, 0 < slack →
    ∀ᶠ d in atTop,
      ∀ Rmass : ℝ,
        ∀ u : EuclideanSpace ℂ (BipIndex (Fin d) (Fin d)),
          Rmass ∈
            betaColumnIntervalSet
              (betaColumnSpikeScale
                (lowerConcreteN d : ℝ) (spikeSpeed k d) a)
              (lowerConcreteDelta a slack d) →
          u ∈
            lowerConcreteDirectionCapSet
              lowerConcreteCanonicalDirection a slack d →
          ‖u‖ = 1 →
          a ^ k - lowerConcreteProfileError k ε a slack d ≤
            columnDirectionSpikeProfile
              (p := Fin d) (q := Fin d)
              (lowerConcreteN d) k Rmass u
```

Current target:

```lean
AppendixB.lower_unitProfile_canonicalDirection_concreteChoices
```

File: `PptFactorization/AristotleTargets/LowerUnitProfileConcreteChoices.lean`.

Plain math meaning: on the Beta lower interval and canonical direction cap, the
pure spike term contributes at least `a^k` up to the concrete `delta` error.

Already available lemmas: Beta interval left endpoint wiring
`lower_betaColumnIntervalSet_left_le`, canonical cap overlap wiring
`lower_canonicalDirection_cap_inner_sq_ge`, and project definitions for
`columnDirectionSpikeProfile`, `betaColumnSpikeScale`,
`lowerConcreteProfileError`, and `lowerConcreteDelta`.

```lean
theorem lower_columnDirectionSpikeProfile_canonicalCap_of_massLower
    {k : ℕ} {ε a slack Rmass : ℝ} {d : ℕ}
    (hk : 1 < k) (hd : 0 < d)
    (ha : spikeRoot k ε < a)

          (lowerConcreteN d : ℝ) (spikeSpeed k d) a ≤ Rmass)
    {u : EuclideanSpace ℂ (BipIndex (Fin d) (Fin d))}
    (hOverlap :
      1 - (1 / (lowerConcreteNcap d : ℝ)) ^ 2 ≤
        ‖inner ℂ
          (coordinateUnitVector
            (ι := BipIndex (Fin d) (Fin d))
            ((⟨0, hd⟩ : Fin d), (⟨0, hd⟩ : Fin d)))
          u‖ ^ 2)
    (hUnit : ‖u‖ = 1) :

```

Expected proof route: expand the spike-profile definitions, use the Beta mass
lower bound and canonical cap overlap, then prove the remaining scalar
inequality against the concrete `delta` budget.

Structural progress: high.  Mathematical hardness: medium.

Done criterion: the pointwise lemma and supplier compile without `sorry`; the
lower endpoint has a wrapper removing `hUnitProfile`; build and audit pass.


Current target:

```lean
AppendixB.lower_scaleLoss_concreteChoices
```

Plain math meaning: replacing the deleted background by its normalized
deleted-column model loses only the concrete scale-error budget.

Already available lemmas: the exact normalization identity
`columnBackgroundContribution_eq_norm_pow_mul_backgroundMomentValue_normalized`
and the definitions of `backgroundTypicalSet`, `sampleColumnComplement`, and
`sampleColumnComplementNormalized`.

First missing local lemma currently isolated:

```lean
theorem lower_backgroundScaleLoss_pointwise_concreteChoices
    (R : _root_.PptFactorization.AppendixB.ConcreteModel.BalancedRegime)
    (k : ℕ) (ε a slack : ℝ) (d : ℕ)
    (hs : 0 < R.sample d)
    (X : SampleMatrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Fin (R.sample d)))
    (hTypical :
      sampleColumnComplementNormalized
          (p := Fin d) (q := Fin d) (σ := Fin (R.sample d))
          X (⟨0, hs⟩ : Fin (R.sample d)) ∈
        backgroundTypicalSet
          (p := Fin d) (q := Fin d) (σ := Fin (R.sample d))
          (lowerConcreteN d) (lowerConcreteM R a slack d)
          (lowerConcreteTau a slack d)
          (lowerConcreteDeletedBackgroundMean R k d) k) :
    backgroundMomentValue
        (p := Fin d) (q := Fin d) (σ := Fin (R.sample d))
        (lowerConcreteN d) k
        (sampleColumnComplementNormalized
          (p := Fin d) (q := Fin d) (σ := Fin (R.sample d))
          X (⟨0, hs⟩ : Fin (R.sample d))) *
      (1 -
        frobeniusNorm
            (p := Fin d) (q := Fin d) (σ := Fin (R.sample d))
            (sampleColumnComplement
              (p := Fin d) (q := Fin d) (σ := Fin (R.sample d))
              X (⟨0, hs⟩ : Fin (R.sample d))) ^ (2 * k)) ≤
        lowerConcreteScaleError R k ε a slack d
```

Important warning: this pointwise target may be too strong as stated for
arbitrary ambient `X`.  The normalized typicality hypothesis controls
`sampleColumnComplementNormalized X α₀`, but not by itself the radial factor
`‖sampleColumnComplement X α₀‖`.  A realistic proof may need to consume the full
favorable event, sphere membership, and Beta mass interval.

Expected proof route: either prove the current pointwise lemma from an existing
sphere/mass normalization fact, or replace it with the smallest correct local
lemma that includes the missing event assumptions and then adapt the supplier.

Structural progress: high.  Mathematical hardness: medium-high.

Done criterion: no over-strong hidden assumption remains; the supplier compiles
without `sorry`; build and audit pass; `hScaleLoss` is no longer visible.
